description: Hernandez is a cardio beast; he performs takedowns non stop and is very good at maintaining ground control to exhaust his opponents. Anthony has very good movement and a balanced, versatile skillset.

fight-history: W vs Max Holloway (U-Dec) R5 5:00 - UFC 326
W vs Mateusz Gamrot (Sub - Rear Naked Choke) R2 2:48 - UFC Fight Night 261
L vs Ilia Topuria (KO/TKO - Strikes) R1 2:27 - UFC 317

description: Charles Do Bronx is a Submission Specialist. Although his durability is not world class due to his diagnosis, he has managed to achieve the Lightweight title at UFC.

Khamzat Chimaev is a wrestling beast. He uses his jab to set up takedowns, and does not mind sitting on his opponent for the whole fight. However, his striking defence is questionable and has had very close fights before because he was not able to takedown his opponent, but he is always improving.
Max Holloway is one of the best boxers in the UFC. He has insane volume output and does not mind taking hits. He is also able to use his boxing continuously due to his very good grappling defence.

fight-history: W vs Anthony Hernandez (KO/TKO - Strikes) R3 2:23 - UFC Fight Night 267

description: Master of distancing himself and the jab, Strickland uses a 'Philly Shell' defence to frustrate and outpoint opponents. He does not like ground play at all and prefers to distance strike the whole fight.

The premier Japanese talent in the UFC and the first fighter born in the 2000s to headline and win a main event. Taira is a submission specialist with arguably the most suffocating top control in the division. His December 2025 TKO win over Brandon Moreno silenced doubters regarding his striking power and has earned him a scheduled title shot against Joshua Van in April 2026.

Royval is the division's premier "chaos" fighter. A BJJ black belt with a high-octane striking style, he often sacrifices defensive positioning to create finishing opportunities and scrambles. Despite recent setbacks against the very top of the division, his elite cardio and "zombie-like" recovery make him one of the most dangerous and entertaining fighters at 125 lbs.

Horiguchi is a true master of distance and timing. A rare hybrid of elite Shotokan Karate and American Top Team wrestling, he possesses arguably the best calf kick in MMA history. After years of dominating RIZIN and Bellator, his 2025-2026 return to the UFC has been flawless, proving that his "blitz" style remains a puzzle that few in the world can solve. He is widely considered the most complete Japanese fighter to ever compete.

Kavanagh is a high-level kickboxer who successfully transitioned his "point-fighting" distance management into elite MMA striking. After a minor setback in 2025, he shocked the world in February 2026 by out-pointing Brandon Moreno over five rounds, utilizing a masterful Outside Sniper approach and near-perfect Defensive Grappling to stay on his feet. He represents the "new breed" of flyweights who prioritize speed, technical precision, and unbreakable takedown defense.

"The Prince" is a master of the "slow burn" fight. He is a world-class grappling specialist who excels at forcing high-level opponents into his world of technical transitions. While his striking is composed and defensively sound (Technical Boxer), his true path to victory is through his All-Round Grappling, where his squeeze and back-taking ability are arguably the best in the division. His recent losses came against the absolute elite, but his victory over Kai Kara-France proved he belongs in the title conversation when healthy.

The first Mexican-born champion in UFC history, Moreno is a high-volume technician with legendary heart. While he is a crisp Technical Boxer on the feet, he is most dangerous in transitions, using his All-Round Grappling to find back-takes and submissions in the middle of scrambles. Despite recent setbacks, his world-class experience and elite-level submission game make him a "gatekeeper to the elite" in the current 2026 landscape.

Perez is a fast-starting veteran known for his elite wrestling background and some of the most punishing calf kicks in the division. While he has struggled with consistency and injuries in recent years, his January 2026 knockout of Charles Johnson proved he still possesses the "reset button" power to compete with anyone in the Top 10. He is a classic All-Round Grappler who uses heavy Pressure Striking to force opponents against the fence.

Tim Elliott is the most unorthodox fighter in the history of the 125lb division. He uses awkward movement, constant head changes, and a relentless pace to break his opponents' rhythm. While he classifies as an All-Round Grappler, his true strength lies in "scramble logic"—winning positions that shouldn't be winnable. As a Pressure Striker, he doesn't look for one-punch KOs but rather uses volume to force clinches and takedowns where he can drown opponents in transitions.

Steve Erceg possesses what many call the "cleanest jab in the division." He is a quintessential Outside Sniper, utilizing elite distance management and a 10/10 jab to dismantle opponents before they can get close. While his striking is his primary weapon, he is a dangerous All-Round Grappler who nearly submitted the champion Pantoja during their title fight. His high Fight-IQ allows him to stay composed in high-pressure situations, though he has recently struggled against the absolute top-tier speed of the division.

Tagir Ulanbekov is a master of the "Dagestani Handcuff" and high-amplitude throws. As an All-Round Grappler, he doesn't just look for takedowns; he looks for total positional dominance. His 10/10 Clinch-Control and Ground-Control allow him to sap the energy of more explosive strikers. While he uses an Outside Sniper style on the feet to stay safe, his primary goal is always to close the distance and initiate the grind. His recent loss to Horiguchi showed a rare vulnerability to high-level veteran savvy, but his previous four-fight win streak cemented him as a permanent fixture in the Top 15.

Merab "The Machine" Dvalishvili is the physical embodiment of relentless pace. He holds the UFC record for most takedowns landed, often attempting dozens in a single fight just to break his opponent's spirit. While he is a Wrestler by trade, he operates as a Pressure Striker on the feet, using high-volume punching purely to mask his entries. His 2025 campaign was legendary—defending his belt against Umar Nurmagomedov and Sean O'Malley—before finally losing a tactical chess match to Petr Yan in their December rematch.

Petr Yan is widely considered the most "complete" striker in the history of the 135-lb division. After a rocky 2022-2023, his 2025 campaign was flawless, culminating in a tactical masterclass where he stuffed 23 of 26 takedown attempts from Merab Dvalishvili. He is a Technical Boxer who uses high-level pressure and a high guard to download his opponent's rhythm before drowning them in the championship rounds. His All-Round Grappling is often underrated; he uses trips and judo-style tosses to supplement his boxing, making him a nightmare to prepare for.

Umar Nurmagomedov is widely considered the most technically versatile member of the Nurmagomedov clan. While he possesses the suffocating All-Round Grappling (10/10 Ground Control) typical of his family, he distinguishes himself with elite-level kickboxing. As an Outside Sniper, he utilizes a devastating 10/10 head kick and complex question-mark kicks to keep opponents at bay. His only career blemish was a hard-fought decision loss to Merab Dvalishvili in 2025, but his recent dominance over Figueiredo and Sandhagen proves he is a champion-in-waiting.

Sean O'Malley remains one of the most prolific and accurate strikers in UFC history. Operating as a world-class Outside Sniper, his ability to manage distance and find "the button" with his cross (10/10 Accuracy) is his greatest asset. While his wrestling was exposed by Merab Dvalishvili in back-to-back losses, O'Malley's recent win over Song Yadong showed a more disciplined, patient version of "Suga." He is currently scheduled to headline the historic UFC Freedom 250 at the White House in June 2026 against the surging Aiemann Zahabi.

Cory Sandhagen is the ultimate "high-entropy" fighter. As a world-class Outside Sniper, he uses constant stance-switching and non-traditional angles to freeze his opponents. He is famous for his flying knees and spinning attacks, but in recent years, he has significantly upgraded his All-Round Grappling, showing a much higher wrestling floor than in his early career. His 10/10 Stamina and Fight-IQ make him a nightmare in five-round main events, as he rarely slows down and constantly adjusts his strategy mid-fight.

fight-history: W vs Deiveson Figueiredo (Sub - Guillotine) R2 4:42 - UFC Fight Night: Song vs. Figueiredo

description: Song Yadong is a physical powerhouse with some of the fastest hands in the history of the 135-lb division. Fighting with a Pressure Boxer style, he relies on a 10/10 Haymaker and a 9/10 Cross to force opponents into defensive shells. In his recent camp at Team Alpha Male, he has significantly improved his All-Round Grappling, using reactive takedowns (8/10) to keep strikers like O'Malley guessing. He is famously durable, possessing a "Granite Chin" (10/10) that has allowed him to trade with the hardest hitters in the world without ever being fully put away in his prime.

Aiemann Zahabi is the definition of "Tristar Discipline." Trained by his brother Firas Zahabi (coach of GSP), Aiemann operates with a perfect 10/10 Fight-IQ. He is a Technical Counter-Striker who refuses to take unnecessary risks, resulting in a 10/10 Striking Defence. He rarely gets hit clean and excels at frustrating aggressive opponents until they make a mistake. While not the most powerful athlete, his 10/10 Accuracy ensures that every shot he lands counts. His recent victory over Marlon Vera proved that his "Slow and Steady" approach can dismantle even the most durable elite contenders.

fight-history: L vs Song Yadong (Sub - Guillotine) R2 4:42 - UFC Fight Night: Song vs. Figueiredo

description: Deiveson Figueiredo is a terrifying finisher who brings championship-level experience and "old man strength" to the 135-pound division. Known for his stone-cold demeanor and bleach-blonde hair, he carries devastating one-punch knockout power and an incredibly opportunistic submission game, particularly his signature guillotine choke. Outside of the cage, Deiveson is a colorful personality who grew up as a buffalo herder and a bricklayer in the Amazon. He is a devoted family man who often travels with his brother, Francisco, and is known for his love of high-fashion outfits and his pet water buffalo back home in Brazil.

Marlon "Chito" Vera is a slow-starting but incredibly violent finisher who represents the pride of Ecuador on the world stage. He fights with a calculated, stoic intensity, often spending the first round "downloading" his opponent's movement before unleashing devastating leg kicks and sharp elbows. Known for having one of the most indestructible chins in the history of the sport, Chito has never been finished in his professional career.

David Martinez is a lethal striking specialist who has quickly become Mexico's brightest hope for a Bantamweight title. A medical doctor outside of the Octagon, he brings a high level of intelligence and "surgical" precision to his fights, utilizing a diverse background in San Da and Kickboxing to maintain range as a clinical Outside Sniper. He is the brother of UFC flyweight Melissa Martinez and is deeply rooted in the tight-knit "Bonebreakers" martial arts community in Mexico City. Despite his high-pressure career in medicine, he maintains an incredibly calm and stoic personality, treating his time in the cage with the same technical focus he applies to his studies.

Payton Talbott is widely considered the "future of the division," possessing a chillingly calm demeanor that stands in stark contrast to his high-volume, violent fighting style. Operating as a relentless Pressure Boxer, he utilizes an almost supernatural gas tank to overwhelm opponents with creative striking and surprisingly effective wrestling, as seen when he out-grappled an Olympic medalist in his last outing. Outside the cage, Talbott is an eclectic and artistic personality who enjoys skating, painting, and fire-spinning, often coming across more like a relaxed Reno local than a cage-fighting phenom. He famously maintains a "low-stress" lifestyle, often crediting his success to not taking the sport—or himself—too seriously, which allows him to stay perfectly composed even in the heat of a high-stakes battle.

Vinicius Oliveira is a pure highlight-reel fighter who approaches every bout with a "kill or be killed" mentality that has made him an instant fan favorite. Known for his explosive athleticism and acrobatic attacks, he fights as a dangerous Outside Sniper who can end a night instantly with flying knees or spinning back-kicks. Outside the Octagon, "Lokomotiva" is known for his boisterous and energetic personality, often arriving at ceremonial weigh-ins with high energy to match his fighting style. He is a proud representative of the Sanda (Chinese Kickboxing) style in MMA, and he carries a deep sense of showmanship, frequently engaging with the crowd and promising "The Knockout of the Year" every time he makes the walk.

Raul Rosas Jr. is a grappling prodigy who carries the confidence and poise of a veteran twice his age. He fights with a relentless Pressure style, using his high-level wrestling and 10th Planet Jiu Jitsu background to overwhelm opponents and force them into exhausting scrambles. Outside the Octagon, Raul is a grounded and deeply family-oriented young man who was famously signed to the UFC at just 17 after a standout performance on Dana White's Contender Series. He is often seen accompanied by his father and brothers, and despite his rapid rise to fame, he remains focused on his goal of becoming the youngest champion in UFC history, a journey he shares openly with his massive following of young fans who see him as the "leader of the new generation."

Montel Jackson is a soft-spoken, highly disciplined athlete who fought his way out of the toughest neighborhoods in Milwaukee to become a world-class martial artist. He fights with a Technical approach that leverages his massive 75.5-inch reach—the longest in the division—allowing him to land "Quik" and devastating straight left hands from a distance. Outside the cage, Jackson is known for his stoic and selfless personality, famously having turned down a college wrestling scholarship in his youth to stay home and care for his sick grandparents. He remains deeply connected to his roots, often mentoring local youth in Milwaukee and crediting his success to a simple philosophy of doing the hard things he doesn't want to do every single day to ensure his growth.

Farid Basharat is a master of "clean" fighting, possessing an elite level of technical discipline that makes him nearly impossible to hit cleanly. Alongside his brother Javid, Farid arrived in England as a refugee from Afghanistan, and that early life of overcoming adversity has translated into a cold, calculated focus inside the cage where he never seems to panic. He is a true intellectual of the sport, spending his time outside the gym analyzing film and maintaining a quiet, professional lifestyle in London. Farid is deeply motivated by the "Basharat vs. The World" mentality, often stating that he and his brother are on a singular mission to prove that their technical, refined style is the most effective way to reach the pinnacle of the sport without taking unnecessary damage.

Alexander Volkanovski is an Australian powerhouse whose transition from semi-professional rugby to mixed martial arts resulted in one of the most dominant championship runs in the history of the Featherweight division. Known for his technical mastery and "unbreakable" mental fortitude, "The Great" utilizes a sophisticated system of feints and elite leg-kicking to dismantle opponents, bolstered by a defensive wrestling base that is arguably the best in the sport's history. After a brief period of adversity, Volkanovski reclaimed his undisputed throne in 2025, showcasing a legendary "second prime" where his elite fight-IQ and relentless pace continue to outclass the division’s newest generation of contenders.

Hailing from Ingushetia, Movsar Evloev is an undefeated force whose tactical brilliance and relentless wrestling have made him the definitive #1 contender in the Featherweight division. A former M-1 Global champion, Evloev utilizes a "Wrestler" style characterized by high-amplitude Greco-Roman takedowns and a suffocating top-game that completely nullifies the offense of his opponents. In his most recent outing in March 2026, he overcame a point deduction to dominate Lerone Murphy in London, proving his elite cardiovascular endurance and ability to remain composed under immense pressure. Evloev is widely considered the ultimate "puzzle" of the 145-pound class, relying on a 10/10 Fight IQ to systematically dismantle adversaries while maintaining a perfect professional record.

Originally from Manaus, Brazil, Diego Lopes moved to Mexico at age 19 to teach Jiu-Jitsu and has since become one of the most beloved figures in Latin American MMA, representing both nations in the Octagon. A 2nd-degree BJJ black belt who began competing at age seven, Lopes is a "Submission" specialist of the highest order, known for attacking limbs and necks from every conceivable position. His 2025 and 2026 campaigns were defined by two epic, "Fight of the Night" title battles against Alexander Volkanovski, where he proved his "Pressure" striking and 10/10 chin could challenge the very best in history. Despite the narrow losses to the champion, Lopes remains a massive superstar and the premier finisher in the Featherweight division, blending terrifying knockout power with a world-class grappling game.

Hailing from Manchester, England, Lerone Murphy earned his nickname "The Miracle" after surviving a near-fatal shooting in 2013, a life-altering event that eventually led him to the world of mixed martial arts. He is an exceptionally disciplined striker who utilizes a long, snapping jab and elite-level lateral movement to keep opponents at the end of his reach. While he entered the UFC as a pure kickboxer, Murphy has evolved into a complete "All-Round" martial artist with a highly underrated defensive wrestling game and a 10/10 Fight IQ. Despite suffering his first professional defeat in a narrow majority decision to Movsar Evloev in March 2026—a fight where he battled through a significant hip injury—Murphy remains a premier European threat in the 145-pound division and a top-tier "Outside Sniper."

personal-info.name: Yair Rodriguez
personal-info.gym: Valle Flow Striking /  Huntington Beach UTC

description: Born and raised in Parral, Chihuahua, Yair Rodriguez grew up in a region often marked by hardship and violence, an environment that forged his resilient and humble personality. He credits his parents as his primary inspirations, maintaining a deep-rooted connection to his Mexican heritage that defines his public persona. Yair is a Taekwondo black belt who began training at age five, and his fighting style is a pure expression of that discipline: he is a highly creative "Outside Sniper" known for acrobatic, high-risk techniques like 360-degree spinning kicks and flying knees. Beyond his flashy striking, he has evolved into a savvy "All-Round" grappler with a dangerous submission game, particularly from his guard. Known for his "El Pantera" moniker, he carries himself with a quiet intensity and a "warrior first" mindset, prioritizing spectacular performances for the fans over safe, point-based victories.

Born in Uniondale, New York, to Jamaican parents, Aljamain Sterling grew up in a large family of twenty siblings, navigating a challenging upbringing by finding sanctuary in wrestling. Known for his "Funk Master" moniker, he earned his stripes as a two-time NCAA Division III All-American before transitioning to MMA, where he famously became the first UFC champion to win a title via disqualification. Personality-wise, Sterling is highly analytical and vocal, often engaging deeply with fans and critics through his own media platforms, showcasing a resilient and business-savvy mindset. His fighting style is defined by an "All-Round" grappling approach that utilizes unorthodox "Funk" wrestling to take backs and secure rear-naked chokes, paired with a "Pressure" striking game that relies heavily on elite-level leg kicks to dictate the range and pace of the fight.

Hailing from Foz do Iguaçu, Brazil, Jean Silva’s journey is one of profound transformation, moving from a difficult upbringing marked by family tragedy and laboring in construction to becoming a global MMA star. A central figure in the "Fighting Nerds" stable, Silva is known for his highly emotional and charismatic personality—often barking like a dog or showing tears of gratitude during his walkouts. He operates under a fierce alter ego he calls "Lord," a persona that manifests as a terrifyingly aggressive "Pressure" striker with elite knockout power in both hands and a lethal clinch game. While his striking garners the headlines, he is a disciplined "All-Round" grappler who recently showcased his evolution with a 2025 "Submission of the Year" contender via ninja choke. Silva balances a quirky, autistic-coded brilliance with a violent, forward-marching style that has made him one of the most bankable and dangerous contenders in the 145-pound division.

Born in Casablanca, Morocco, Youssef Zalal’s introduction to combat was born of necessity; his mother enrolled him in kickboxing at age ten to channel his energy away from the frequent street fights of his neighborhood. After moving to the United States at fifteen, he discovered MMA almost by accident, initially believing "cage fighting" was a fight to the death before falling in love with the technicality of the sport. Zalal is a deeply disciplined and resilient individual who publicly documented the mental "hurt" of being released from the UFC in 2022, using that period of reflection to reinvent himself into a more aggressive finisher. His fighting style has evolved into that of a premier "Submission" specialist, utilizing his long limbs to snatch necks and arms with lightning speed, while maintaining the "Outside Sniper" striking foundation from his youth. Outside the gym, he is a self-described "car obsessive" who uses specific goals—like purchasing his dream car—to fuel his "African mentality" of never stopping until the mission is complete.

Raised in a coastal shipping port in Suffolk, England, Arnold Allen grew up in a household built for physical grit; his father and brother were both professional strongmen, a background that instilled in him a quiet, blue-collar toughness. Eschewing the family trend of lifting stones, Allen began his MMA journey as a teenager, even sleeping on gym floors during his early career to pursue his UFC dream. He is a humble, soft-spoken individual who prefers the quiet life in Felixstowe over the bright lights of MMA stardom, often seen as the ultimate "professional's professional." His fighting style is that of a surgical "Outside Sniper," utilizing a lightning-fast southpaw straight and world-class defensive movement that makes him one of the hardest fighters in the division to clean land on. While his striking is his primary weapon, he is a black belt in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu and a highly disciplined "All-Round" grappler, capable of stifling elite wrestlers or snatching submissions in the heat of a scramble.

Born and raised in Albuquerque, New Mexico, Steve Garcia is a product of the legendary Jackson-Wink MMA stable, embodying the grit and high-altitude conditioning of the high desert. Known as the "Mean Machine," his personality is a stark contrast to his moniker; he is a deeply family-oriented man and a devout Christian who views his fighting career as a platform for his faith and a way to provide for his wife and children. His fighting style is defined by a terrifying "Pressure" game, where he utilizes his massive 6'0" frame and southpaw power to overwhelm opponents with heavy-handed volume. Garcia is a specialist in "Ground and Pound," often using his long reach to hurt opponents on the feet before finishing them with brutal elbows and strikes on the mat. Despite early career setbacks at Bantamweight, his 2022–2026 run at Featherweight has established him as a premier finisher who thrives in chaotic brawls and possesses a remarkable ability to recover from early damage to secure a stoppage.

personal-info.name: Kevin Vallejos
personal-info.gym: Team Lucas Mineiro / Brothers of Life MMA

description: Born in Mar del Plata, Argentina, Kevin Vallejos represents the vanguard of a new, highly aggressive wave of South American talent. Given the nickname "El Chino" by his first coach, he carries a humble and deeply focused personality, often cited by his team for his obsession with technical perfection and a remarkably calm demeanor under fire. His fighting style is that of a devastating "Pressure" striker; he is a specialist in explosive power shots, famously holding one of the few spinning back-fist knockouts in UFC history. While his striking is his primary calling card, he has developed a robust "All-Round" grappling game, maintaining a high takedown defense rate that forces opponents to stand and trade with him. As the youngest fighter in the Top 10, Vallejos has become an overnight sensation in Argentina, blending a charismatic public image with a violent, finishing-oriented approach that has drawn comparisons to a young Ilia Topuria.

Born in Los Angeles to Mexican parents from Hermosillo, Sonora, Brian Ortega grew up in the projects of San Pedro, a childhood he often cites as the source of his resilience and "never die" attitude. Known as "T-City" (Triangle City) for his early mastery of the triangle choke, Ortega is a Gracie Jiu-Jitsu black belt whose personality is characterized by a calm, soft-spoken nature and a deep commitment to his community and youth programs. His fighting style is that of an elite "Submission" specialist who is arguably the most dangerous finisher in the world once a fight hits the floor or a clinch scramble begins. While he initiated his career as a pure grappler, he evolved into a dangerous "Outside Sniper" with deceptive power, famously becoming the first man to knock out the legendary Frankie Edgar. Ortega is renowned for his granite chin and supernatural recovery, often surviving immense damage to snatch a victory from the jaws of defeat in the closing minutes of a fight.

Born in Belém, Brazil, Melquizael Costa’s life has been defined by his battle with vitiligo, an autoimmune condition that caused him significant social isolation and bullying throughout his childhood. This adversity led him to martial arts as a means of building self-confidence, eventually adopting the nickname "The Dalmatian" to embrace the very traits that once made him a target of discrimination. Before finding success in the UFC, Costa worked as a popsicle seller on the streets of Brazil to support his training, a period that instilled in him a profound sense of humility and work ethic. His fighting style is that of a highly technical "Outside Sniper," specializing in creative, high-impact kicks and lightning-fast southpaw counters. Despite his striking prowess, he is an elite "Submission" specialist who often uses his long frame to lock in guillotines and chokes with precision. Costa is widely viewed as a source of inspiration for fans worldwide, combining a resilient personality with a violent, highlight-reel finishing ability that has propelled him into the featherweight elite.

personal-info.name: Patricio Freire
personal-info.fighting-out-of: Natal, Rio Grande do Norte, Brazil

description: Born in Mossoró and fighting out of Natal, Brazil, Patrício "Pitbull" Freire is widely regarded as the greatest fighter in Bellator history, having held championships in two weight classes simultaneously. He co-founded the "Pitbull Brothers" gym with his brother Patricky, creating a family-oriented but fiercely competitive environment that has produced several world-class athletes. Patrício is known for his stoic, intense personality and a "king-like" presence in the cage, often appearing shorter than his opponents but neutralizing them with extreme technical precision. His fighting style is that of a master "Counter" striker, possessing legendary one-punch knockout power and some of the best calf kicks in the sport's history. Despite his elite striking, he is an "All-Round" grappling threat and a Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu black belt who specializes in the guillotine choke. As he enters the final chapter of his career in the UFC, Freire remains a cerebral tactician who relies on a 10/10 Fight IQ to overcome larger, younger opponents.

Born in the Kyangwali Refugee Settlement in Uganda before relocating to the United States, David Onama’s life is a testament to perseverance and the pursuit of the American dream. Known as the "Silent Assassin," he carries a reserved and stoic personality, preferring to let his clinical performances inside the Octagon speak for his ambition. His fighting style is rooted in a highly technical "Outside Sniper" approach, characterized by one of the most effective and educated jabs in the division, which he uses to set up explosive power shots. While he is primarily recognized for his striking, Onama is a versatile "All-Round" grappler who has secured multiple high-level submissions, often surprising opponents who attempt to take him down. He is a fan-favorite for his willingness to take fights on short notice and his tendency to engage in "Fight of the Night" wars, embodying a resilient spirit forged through his early life challenges.

Growing up in a dysfunctional home in Phoenix, Arizona, before moving to Sacramento, Josh Emmett used wrestling as a stabilizing force throughout his youth, eventually competing at the collegiate level. A student of psychology with a Bachelor's degree from Menlo College, Emmett is known for his incredibly disciplined, blue-collar personality and a level of professional loyalty rarely seen in the sport, having remained with Team Alpha Male for his entire career. His fighting style is that of a nuclear "Counter" striker; he possesses arguably the greatest one-punch knockout power in Featherweight history, capable of ending any fight with a single overhand right. Despite his reliance on his "touch of death," he remains an elite-level "Wrestler" with a 14-year background in the sport, typically using those skills defensively to keep the fight standing where he can hunt for the finish. Now the elder statesman of the Top 15, Emmett is revered for his grit and his ability to remain a championship-level threat well into his late 30s and early 40s.

Born in Germany to Georgian parents and raised in Alicante, Spain, Ilia Topuria possesses a cosmopolitan identity and a supreme confidence that has defined his meteoric rise. A Greco-Roman wrestler since age seven and a Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu black belt, "El Matador" is a rare example of a fighter who transitioned from a submission specialist to one of the most feared "Outside Sniper" strikers in the sport. Personality-wise, he is intensely focused and manifest-driven, famously predicting his own knockouts and title wins with eerie accuracy. His style is built on boxing fundamentals that are technically perfect, utilizing a 10/10 defense to stay safe while hunting for a single, fight-ending counter-hook. Despite his world-class "All-Round" grappling, he has increasingly relied on his hands, becoming the first man in history to knock out both Alexander Volkanovski and Max Holloway, cementing his place as a generational pound-for-pound talent.

Justin Gaethje is the most violent "Pressure" striker in the history of the Lightweight division. Raised in a mining family in Arizona, he carries a blue-collar "work until the job is done" mentality into the cage, which manifests as an unbreakable will to endure damage in exchange for landing his own. While he was an NCAA Division I All-American wrestler, Gaethje has famously abandoned offensive grappling in the UFC, choosing instead to use his wrestling base as a 10/10 defensive wall to keep fights on the feet. His personality is defined by total honesty and a lack of ego; he views MMA as a "car crash" and accepts the risks of his style with a stoic, warrior-like calm. In the cage, he is a master of the low calf kick, using it to deaden his opponent's movement before finishing them with terrifyingly powerful hooks. Under the tutelage of Trevor Wittman, he has transitioned from a reckless brawler into a more patient "Highlight" reel, though he remains the division's ultimate litmus test for heart and chin.

Born in a small Armenian-populated town in Georgia and raised in Russia, Arman Tsarukyan is the son of a successful construction businessman, which afforded him a disciplined and well-resourced upbringing. A former competitive ice hockey player and a Master of Sports in Freestyle Wrestling, "Ahalkalakets" (a nickname referencing his birthplace) is defined by a relentless, analytical personality and a supreme belief that he is the best grappler in the world. His fighting style is the gold standard for "Pressure" wrestling in 2026. He uses a high-level kicking game to force opponents back toward the fence, where he initiates explosive takedown chains. Once on the mat, his 10/10 ground control and scrambling ability make him almost impossible to shake off. Unlike many wrestlers, Tsarukyan is a finisher; he demonstrated this in November 2025 by systematically breaking down Dan Hooker before securing a clinical arm-triangle choke in the second round. He is currently the #1 ranked contender and has been vocal about his desire to "save" the division from what he calls "inactive champions."

Benoît Saint Denis is a modern-day gladiator whose fighting style is an extension of his background in the French Special Forces (1st Marine Infantry Parachute Regiment). His personality is stoic, intensely patriotic, and deeply rooted in a warrior ethos; he famously fought through a massive staph infection and a near-knockout against Dustin Poirier before eventually succumbing, a performance that actually raised his stock due to his sheer durability.In the Octagon, BSD is a "Pressure" specialist who utilizes a heavy Southpaw kicking game—specifically a 10/10 body kick—to collapse his opponent's cardio. While his striking is violent, he is a black belt in Judo and a high-level Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu practitioner, often using the "Clinch" to transition into dominant grappling exchanges. He does not "spar" with opponents; he initiates a war of attrition, banking on the fact that his "God of War" stamina and chin will outlast the person standing across from him. As of 2026, he is the undisputed face of French MMA.

Hailing from the Huyton area of Liverpool, Paddy Pimblett is a generational charisma who has transcended the sport to become a mainstream celebrity in the UK. He is known for his boisterous, unapologetic Scouse personality and his outspoken advocacy for men's mental health, a cause he championed following the tragic loss of a friend. Despite facing constant criticism regarding his weight fluctuations between training camps, he has consistently proven his detractors wrong by showing up in peak condition for his most significant challenges. In the cage, he is a dangerous grappler with a black belt in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, specializing in back takes and opportunistic chokes. While his striking was once considered his primary weakness due to a tendency to keep his chin exposed, his 2025 knockout of Michael Chandler showcased a vastly improved power game. He relies on a high-octane style that prioritizes finishing the fight over winning rounds on the cards. Though his seven-fight UFC win streak was snapped by Justin Gaethje in an interim title war, his performance solidified him as a legitimate elite contender rather than just a hype train.

Mateusz Gamrot is a former two-division KSW champion and one of the most decorated freestyle wrestlers to ever transition into the UFC Lightweight division. Born in Bielsko-Biała, Poland, "Gamer" is known for his incredibly humble and professional demeanor, often cited by coaches at American Top Team as one of the hardest workers in the room. His fighting style is defined by a relentless, high-pace grappling system that relies on world-class chain wrestling and nearly bottomless cardio. He specializes in "scrambling," a chaotic phase of grappling where his ability to transition between positions often leaves opponents exhausted and outmatched. While he has developed a respectable technical boxing game used primarily to set up his entries, his core identity remains rooted in his wrestling dominance. Despite a setback against Charles Oliveira in October 2025, where he was caught in a face crank, Gamrot remains a "boogeyman" of the division who has already secured wins over other elite contenders like Arman Tsarukyan and Rafael Fiziev.

Maurício Ruffy is the breakout star of the "Fighting Nerds" stable in São Paulo, recognizable by the signature nerd glasses he wears outside the cage. His personality is a contrast of calm, intellectual professionalism and terrifying "One Shot" explosive power, earning him a reputation as one of the most dangerous strikers to enter the division in years. His fighting style is that of a surgical "Outside Sniper," utilizing a 75-inch reach to keep opponents at the end of a stinging jab before uncorking high-level spinning attacks or piston-like straight rights. While he was out-grappled in his 2025 loss to Benoît Saint Denis, his January 2026 victory over Rafael Fiziev showcased a more mature, disciplined version of his game where he successfully defended takedowns to keep the fight in his preferred striking range. As he approaches his June 2026 clash with Michael Chandler, Ruffy is widely viewed as the future of the division and a potential champion who possesses the rare "it factor" required for global superstardom.

Renato Moicano has transformed from a quiet featherweight contender into one of the most entertaining and outspoken personalities in the Lightweight division. Born in Brasília, Brazil, the "Money Moicano" persona emerged from his viral post-fight interviews where he discusses everything from his love of the U.S. Constitution to his desire for massive paydays. His fighting style is a high-level blend of technical boxing and world-class Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, specializing in taking the back and finishing with a rear-naked choke. While his striking has improved significantly under the tutelage of American Top Team, his core strength remains his offensive grappling and his ability to find a finish once the fight hits the canvas. Despite a devastating TKO loss to Benoît Saint Denis in late 2025 that halted his momentum toward the top five, Moicano remains a dangerous veteran who can submit anyone in the world if the fight gets to the ground.

Michael Chandler is a former three-time Bellator Lightweight Champion and one of the most explosive athletes to ever compete in the UFC. Known for his high-energy "See You at the Top" personality and motivational speaking, Chandler has become a massive fan favorite for his willingness to prioritize entertainment over a safe path to victory. His fighting style is built on a foundation of All-American wrestling, but in the UFC he has primarily operated as a nuclear "Pressure Striker," often abandoning his grappling to chase highlight-reel knockouts. He possesses fight-ending power in both hands and an elite level of explosiveness that makes the first round of his fights some of the most dangerous minutes in the sport. Despite a difficult 2025 where he suffered a dominant loss to Arman Tsarukyan, Chandler remains a top-tier attraction and a dangerous veteran presence as he prepares to test the surging Maurício Ruffy in June 2026.

Rafael Fiziev is a world-class Muay Thai specialist from Azerbaijan who has become a human highlight reel since joining the UFC. A former police officer and tactical instructor, "Ataman" is known for his playful and humorous personality outside the cage, often engaging in lighthearted banter with opponents and fans alike. His fighting style is arguably the most technical striking game in the division, characterized by blinding speed, creative combinations, and a unique "Matrix" like ability to lean back and dodge strikes by centimeters. While he is a 0.95 ratio striker who rarely initiates grappling, his takedown defense is elite, allowing him to force opponents into the standing wars he prefers. Following a devastating knee injury in late 2023 and back-to-back losses to Gaethje and the surging Ruffy, Fiziev is currently in a transitional phase of his career, working to reclaim his spot as the most dangerous kickboxer in the 155-pound division.

Jalin Turner is a physical anomaly in the Lightweight division, standing at a towering 191 cm with a massive 77-inch reach that earns him his "Tarantula" nickname. His personality is remarkably laid-back and soft-spoken for a man who possesses some of the most violent one-shot power in the 155-pound category. He is a pure "Sniper" who excels at using his length to land devastating straight lefts and knees, often finishing opponents before they can even find their range. While he is a capable grappler with a dangerous guillotine choke, he has struggled with high-level wrestlers and pressure fighters who can navigate his reach. His recent split-decision loss to Dan Hooker in their late 2025 rematch was a "Fight of the Year" contender that showed his heart, but solidified his role as a dangerous high-level gatekeeper in the current rankings.

Beneil Dariush is one of the most respected and technically proficient mixed martial artists in the world, known for a personality defined by deep religious faith and a remarkably humble, soft-spoken nature. An elite Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu black belt under Romulo Barral, Dariush is a "Submission Specialist" whose style is built on suffocating top pressure and an uncanny ability to find neck exposures during transitions. While he possesses a dangerous Muay Thai game developed at Kings MMA, his primary path to victory involves neutralizing his opponent’s movement through world-class clinch work and grappling. Though he has faced a difficult run against the absolute top of the division recently—including a knockout loss to Benoît Saint Denis in late 2025—his technical mastery and "Fight-IQ" make him a perennial threat to anyone in the Lightweight rankings.

Farès Ziam is a rising representative of the surging French MMA scene, known for his calm, methodical approach and high-level kickboxing pedigree. His personality is understated and professional; he is widely regarded as a "gym rat" who has made massive technical leaps since moving his training camps to Sanford MMA in Florida. His fighting style is that of a disciplined "Outside Sniper," using his 185 cm frame and 75-inch reach to maintain distance and pick apart opponents with a laser-like jab and diverse kicking game. While he struggled with high-level wrestling early in his UFC career, he has developed a robust 0.85 ratio striking system that relies on elite defensive movement to keep the fight standing. His recent 2025 victory over the durable Drew Dober proved he can compete with the division's heavy hitters, marking him as the dark horse of the Lightweight Top 15 heading into mid-2026.

Islam Makhachev is the current Pound-for-Pound king and the successor to the legacy of Khabib Nurmagomedov. Known for his disciplined, stoic, and deeply religious personality, Islam carries the weight of Dagestani MMA with a calm confidence. His fighting style is the pinnacle of "Sambo-based" MMA, utilizing high-amplitude trips from the clinch and a suffocating top-control game that makes it nearly impossible for opponents to regain their feet. Unlike many wrestlers, his striking is exceptionally efficient; he holds one of the highest strike-defense percentages in UFC history, rarely taking clean damage while setting up his takedown entries with a sharp, southpaw check-right hook. Since capturing the Welterweight title in November 2025, Islam has been sidelined with a minor hand injury, but he is currently the target of every top 170-pounder, with Ian Machado Garry and Shavkat Rakhmonov leading the pack for a title shot in late 2026.

Jack Della Maddalena is the premier technical boxer of the Welterweight division and the first Western Australian to hold a UFC undisputed title, which he briefly held in 2025. Known for his stoic, "all-business" personality, Jack is a soft-spoken family man who avoids the typical trash talk of the sport, letting his incredibly high-level striking do the talking. His style is built around a 10/10 technical jab and some of the best body-punching seen in MMA history, which he uses to systematically break down his opponents' defense and cardio. While his defensive wrestling has improved significantly, his late 2025 loss to Islam Makhachev highlighted the gap between elite striking and generational Dagestani grappling, as he was controlled on the mat for the majority of the five-round bout. Now ranked #1, JDM is currently scheduled to headline a massive "homecoming" Fight Night in Perth in May 2026 against the surging Carlos Prates to determine the next true challenger for the throne.

Ian Machado Garry is the torchbearer for Irish MMA in the post-McGregor era, carrying a persona defined by supreme self-confidence and a polarizing, headline-grabbing public life. Despite facing significant online scrutiny regarding his personal relationships and gym hopping, Garry has remained remarkably poised inside the Octagon, demonstrating a "Fight-IQ" that is arguably the highest in the division. His fighting style is that of a highly mobile "Technical Sniper," utilizing his massive 191 cm frame to maintain a range where he can land a 10/10 jab and devastating long-range head kicks. While his undefeated record was snapped by Shavkat Rakhmonov in a 2024 title eliminator, his 2025 campaign was flawless, punctuated by a tactical masterclass against Carlos Prates and a dominant decision over Belal Muhammad in Qatar. Now training out of Chute Boxe in Brazil, Garry has integrated more aggression into his game and is currently campaigning for a title shot against Islam Makhachev, claiming his height and movement make him the champion's stylistic nightmare.

Michael Morales is the crown jewel of Ecuadorian MMA and one of the most physically imposing athletes in the 170-pound division. Born to parents who were both high-level judokas, Morales possesses a natural athletic pedigree that translates into freakish functional strength and a nearly impenetrable 89% takedown defense. His personality is marked by a quiet, humble intensity; he rarely engages in trash talk, preferring to let his undefeated record and violent finishing rate speak for itself. In the Octagon, he is a "Power Boxer" who utilizes a massive 79-inch reach to uncork some of the fastest and heaviest hands in the division. His 2025 campaign was arguably the best in UFC history for a rising contender, scoring back-to-back first-round knockouts over established veterans Gilbert Burns and Sean Brady. As of March 2026, he is the only undefeated fighter in the Welterweight top five and is widely considered the "dark horse" who has the physical tools to actually push the champion, Islam Makhachev, in a pure wrestling exchange.

Belal Muhammad is a former UFC Welterweight Champion who reached the mountaintop in July 2024 by defeating Leon Edwards in a dominant wrestling display. Known for his "Remember The Name" mantra and high-level social media engagement, Belal is a cerebral fighter who often thrives on being the underdog. His fighting style is built on relentless forward pressure and elite "Chain Wrestling," where he uses a high-volume jab to close the distance and transition into exhausting ground control. While he finally secured the title, his reign was short-lived, as he lost the belt to Jack Della Maddalena in mid-2025. Following a subsequent technical decision loss to Ian Machado Garry in late 2025, Belal is currently in a "must-win" position as he prepares for a June 2026 clash against the dangerous Gabriel Bonfim to prove he still belongs in the title conversation.

Quillan Salkilld is a rising Australian lightweight prospect out of Luistro Combat Academy in Perth. A former Eternal MMA Lightweight Champion, he burst onto the UFC scene with a 19-second KO on debut and has gone 4-0 in the octagon, earning three Performance of the Night bonuses. He is a well-rounded finisher with devastating head kicks and reliable submission grappling backed by a black belt in BJJ.

Carlos Prates is the second half of the "Fighting Nerds" invasion of the UFC rankings, earning his "Nightmare" nickname because promoters in Brazil literally could not find anyone willing to fight him. A lifelong Muay Thai practitioner with nearly 100 striking matches, Prates has brought a level of terrifying precision to the Welterweight division that is rarely seen. His fighting style centers on a "Power-Sniper" approach, utilizing a massive 78-inch reach and a lightning-fast straight left to shut opponents' lights out. His 2025 campaign was spectacular, highlighted by a brutal knockout of former champ Leon Edwards at UFC 322. Although he suffered a narrow decision loss to Ian Machado Garry earlier that year, Prates’ ability to finish fights in the first or second round makes him the most avoided man in the Top 5. He is currently set for the biggest fight of his life: a May 2026 main event in Australia against Jack Della Maddalena.

Sean Brady is the premier submission threat of the Welterweight division, a Philadelphia native whose personality is defined by a blue-collar work ethic and a "Philly Tough" attitude. He is a high-level Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu black belt who specializes in a suffocating top-pressure game, often referred to as "The Human Wet Blanket" for his ability to neutralize opponents once he secures a takedown. His 2025 campaign was historic, as he became the first man to submit former champion Leon Edwards, catching him in a fourth-round guillotine. However, his momentum was halted in November 2025 at UFC 322, where he suffered a brutal first-round knockout at the hands of Michael Morales. Despite that setback, Brady remains one of the most avoided grapplers in the top ten and is currently booked for a high-stakes May 2026 clash against Joaquin Buckley at UFC 328 to regain his title-contender status.

Kamaru Usman is one of the greatest Welterweights to ever step into the Octagon, a former long-reigning champion whose legacy is built on a foundation of dominant wrestling and an ever-evolving striking game. His personality is that of a seasoned veteran and mentor, often appearing as an analyst when not competing, though he still harbors deep championship ambitions. After a difficult three-fight skid that saw him lose his belt and drop a short-notice middleweight war to Khamzat Chimaev, Usman made a triumphant return to 170 lbs in June 2025. He dismantled the surging Joaquin Buckley over five rounds in a "Fight of the Night" performance that proved his wrestling and cardio are still elite. Now aged 38, Usman is currently eyeing a massive cross-generational legacy fight against a returning Conor McGregor in late 2026, a bout that would likely serve as his final push toward reclaiming the gold.

Leon Edwards is the former UFC Welterweight Champion whose "Rocky" story reached its peak with his legendary head-kick knockout of Kamaru Usman. Known for a calm, stoic, and deeply professional personality, Edwards has often been characterized as a "forgotten man" in the division despite his elite technical skills. His fighting style is built on extreme precision and arguably the best clinch-work in the sport, utilizing his long levers to out-position and out-strike opponents from the outside. However, 2025 was a brutal year for the Birmingham native; after losing his title to Belal Muhammad, he suffered a submission loss to Sean Brady and was most recently knocked out by a Carlos Prates straight left at UFC 322. Now on a three-fight skid for the first time in his career, Edwards is looking to completely reinvent his approach for a potential "redemption run" starting in late 2026.

Joaquin Buckley is a walking highlight reel who has become one of the most active and vocal fighters in the UFC. Possessing a boisterous and highly marketable personality, "New Mansa" is a master of the call-out, often using his post-fight interviews to demand the biggest names in the sport. His fighting style is defined by terrifying explosiveness and unusual physical dimensions for a welterweight, carrying 185-pound power in a compact frame with a deceptive 76-inch reach. After a massive 2024 campaign where he knocked out Stephen Thompson and out-worked Colby Covington, his momentum was slowed in June 2025 by a technical masterclass from Kamaru Usman. Despite that loss, Buckley remains one of the division's most dangerous "wildcards." He is currently scheduled to headline a high-stakes Fight Night against #6 Sean Brady in April 2026, a bout that many see as a final eliminator to enter the top-five elite.

Gabriel Bonfim is a "Submission Specialist" with a terrifying killer instinct, currently holding one of the highest finish rates in the division. Alongside his brother Ismael, Gabriel represents a new wave of Brazilian talent that combines high-level athleticism with elite Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu. His personality is one of quiet intensity; he is a stoic finisher who rarely celebrates for long before looking for his next target. In the Octagon, he is famous for his "Guillotine" and "D'Arce" chokes, which he can snatch in a split second during any scramble. After a lone setback against Nicolas Dalby in 2023, Bonfim has been perfect, most recently scoring a dominant TKO over Randy Brown in late 2025. He is currently scheduled for the biggest opportunity of his career: a June 6, 2026 main event against former champion Belal Muhammad, a fight that could catapult him into the Top 5.

Gilbert "Durinho" Burns is a multi-time BJJ World Champion and one of the most beloved figures in MMA due to his "anyone, anywhere" attitude. Known for his infectious smile and immense heart, Burns has spent years as the ultimate litmus test for the division's elite. While he is a 10/10 grappler, he has developed a "Power Boxer" style that has seen him drop legends like Kamaru Usman and Khamzat Chimaev. However, as of March 2026, the 39-year-old veteran is facing the toughest stretch of his career, currently on a four-fight losing streak against the current Top 5. Despite the losses, he remains a massive threat on the ground and a highly respected veteran. He is set to fight Mike Malott on April 18, 2026, in a high-pressure "gatekeeper" bout where a loss might signal the end of his time in the rankings.

Uroš Medić is the dark horse of the division, a Serbian "Power Kickboxer" who has earned a reputation for being one of the most clinical finishers in the UFC. His personality is calm and analytical, earning him "The Doctor" nickname for the surgical way he dismantles opponents. Since moving permanently to Welterweight, Medić has looked unstoppable; his 2025 campaign featured three consecutive first-round knockouts, culminating in a violent 79-second starching of veteran Geoff Neal in February 2026. While his defensive wrestling was historically a question mark, his training at Kings MMA has shored up his sprawl, allowing him to keep fights standing where his 11 knockout wins prove he is one of the most dangerous men on the roster.

Yaroslav Amosov is a four-time Combat Sambo World Champion and the former undisputed Bellator Welterweight Champion. His personality is defined by an unbreakable quiet resolve; he famously paused his MMA career in 2022 to defend his hometown of Irpin during the Russian invasion of Ukraine, even recovering his championship belt from his home after it was liberated. In the Octagon, Amosov is a physical nightmare for anyone not named Islam Makhachev. His fighting style centers on "relentless chain wrestling" and a gas tank that seems bottomless. He doesn't just look for one takedown; he forces a constant scramble until he finds a dominant position or a neck. His UFC debut in December 2025 proved he belongs at the very top, as he became one of the few men to finish Neil Magny on the ground via anaconda choke. Now ranked #13, Amosov is widely viewed as the most dangerous dark horse in the division—a man with the Sambo credentials to potentially match the champion's grappling.

Mike Malott is the face of the modern Canadian MMA resurgence, a highly articulate and charismatic fighter who previously served as a coach at Team Alpha Male before deciding to pursue his own championship dreams. His personality is one of extreme professionalism and altruism; he famously donated his entire show purse to a teammate's family in need, endearing him to the global fan base. In the Octagon, Malott is a "Technical" striker with a diverse arsenal, but he is most dangerous as a "Submission" specialist. He possesses a lightning-fast guillotine and a high-level transitional grappling game that has resulted in a 100% finish rate across all his professional wins. After a heartbreaking late-round TKO loss to Neil Magny in early 2024 that served as a massive learning experience, Malott returned in late 2025 with a clinical performance that vaulted him into the Top 15. He is currently preparing for a legacy-defining bout against Gilbert Burns in April 2026.

Daniel Rodriguez is a gritty, late-bloomer who has built his career on being one of the most reliable and technically sound boxers in the UFC. His personality is one of a "tough-as-nails" veteran who is willing to take any fight on short notice, a trait that has earned him immense respect from both the promotion and the fans. "D-Rod" operates as a Technical striker in the southpaw stance, utilizing a piston-like, 10/10 jab and high-accuracy straight lefts to win wars of attrition. While his wrestling is purely defensive, his All-Round grappling has proven enough to keep him upright against most. After a rough patch in late 2024, Rodriguez completely revitalized his career with a 3-fight win streak, capped off by a technical masterpiece against Kevin Holland in 2025. At 39 years old, he serves as the final gatekeeper of the rankings, testing the durability of any prospect trying to enter the elite level.

Nassourdine Imavov is the premier technician of the Middleweight division, representing the elite MMA Factory in Paris. He possesses a calm, stoic personality and a cerebral approach to combat that often frustrates more aggressive opponents. His fighting style is a masterclass in Technical striking; he uses his massive 191 cm frame to maintain a long-range "Sniper" game, utilizing a 10/10 straight cross that is arguably the cleanest in the weight class. While he hails from a Dagestani background, he employs an All-Round grappling system primarily for defensive purposes, ensuring the fight stays where he can pick opponents apart. Following a dominant 2025 campaign where he out-pointed Jared Cannonier and Brendan Allen, Imavov has solidified himself as the #1 contender. He is currently waiting for the winner of Chimaev vs. Strickland to claim his first shot at undisputed gold.

Israel Adesanya is a two-time Middleweight champion and one of the most prolific strikers in UFC history. Known for his "Last Stylebender" persona and anime-inspired walkouts, his personality is that of a creative showman who thrives on the big stage. His fighting style is the gold standard for an Outside Sniper; he uses his 80-inch reach and "Switch" stance to create angles that few can navigate, backed by 10/10 technical accuracy. While his offensive wrestling is non-existent, his Defensive grappling has been the backbone of his long reign, boasting an elite 76% takedown defense rate. However, 2024 and 2025 were the hardest years of his career, marked by a three-fight losing skid that saw him lose his belt and get finished by Imavov. As of March 2026, many are questioning if the 36-year-old still has the "hunger," a question he looks to answer this weekend in a main-event clash against the young powerhouse Joe Pyfer.

Caio Borralho is the primary representative of the "Fighting Nerds" camp, a stable that has taken the UFC by storm with their calculated, data-driven approach to fighting. His personality is that of a calm scholar of the game; he is rarely seen without his glasses outside the cage and approaches every opponent as a puzzle to be solved. His fighting style is a disciplined Technical game from the southpaw stance, where he maintains an incredibly high 10/10 accuracy. On March 7, 2026, at UFC 326, he successfully bounced back from his first UFC loss by neutralizing the dangerous grappling of Reinier de Ridder over three rounds. Borralho’s All-Round grappling defense remains his greatest asset, allowing him to dictate exactly where the fight takes place. Now firmly at #5, he has called out former champion Dricus Du Plessis for a high-stakes eliminator later this year.

Reinier de Ridder is the former two-division ONE Championship king who took the UFC by storm in 2025. His personality is one of quiet, supreme confidence, often carrying the poise of a "Dutch Knight." His fighting style is built on a world-class Submission game that utilizes his massive 78-inch reach to find chokes from impossible angles. While his Technical striking is primarily a means to enter the clinch, his 10/10 clinch-control allows him to drag almost anyone to the mat. In 2025, he shocked the world by submitting Bo Nickal and out-grappling Robert Whittaker. However, his recent run has hit a wall; after a TKO loss to Brendan Allen, he was most recently out-pointed by Caio Borralho at UFC 326. Despite the two-fight skid, he remains the most dangerous BJJ threat in the division.

Gregory Rodrigues, the "Robocop," is currently the division's most exciting action fighter. His personality is humble and deeply religious, but his fighting style is pure Muay Thai / Kickboxing violence. On March 7, 2026, he avenged a previous loss by knocking out Brunno Ferreira in less than two minutes. While his chin (7/10) has failed him in the past, his 10/10 recovery is legendary; he often gets wobbled only to storm back and finish his opponent. Now at #10, he has officially called out Anthony Hernandez for a summer showdown.

Jared Cannonier stands as one of the most physically imposing and disciplined athletes in the history of the sport, having successfully navigated a career path that saw him transform from a heavyweight into a shredded, top-tier middleweight contender. He carries a stoic and deeply philosophical personality, often speaking about the importance of energy, frequency, and mental fortitude, which manifests in the cage as a terrifyingly focused and unwavering presence. His approach to combat is built on relentless forward movement and a heavy-handed boxing game that is punctuated by some of the most bone-shattering leg kicks in the division. While he possesses the raw strength to knockout anyone with a single connection, he has also developed the deep gas tank necessary to maintain a record-breaking pace over five rounds. Defensively, he relies on his immense physical power to shuck off grappling attempts and keep the fight in a standing position where he can dictate the terms of the engagement. Entering 2026, the veteran remains a critical hurdle for any rising contender, as seen in his recent wars where he demonstrated that despite his age, his durability and ability to absorb damage while returning fire remain elite. Though he has faced recent setbacks against the division's younger technicians, his ability to outwork top-tier opponents like Marvin Vettori and starch power-hitters like Gregory Rodrigues keeps him firmly cemented in the upper echelon of the rankings as he looks for a significant legacy-defining matchup to close out his storied career.

Michel Pereira is a captivating and unpredictable force within the middleweight division, renowned for a flamboyant personality that often blends elite martial arts with theatrical showmanship. He approaches each bout as an opportunity to entertain, frequently utilizing a high-energy attacking system that favors explosive impact over steady volume. His offensive game is anchored by a diverse arsenal of strikes where he predominantly relies on heavy-handed boxing and devastating hooks, though he remains a constant threat with acrobatic maneuvers like flying knees and spinning attacks that keep his opponents in a state of defensive hesitation. Despite his reputation for chaos on the feet, he possesses a remarkably solid physical foundation that allows him to excel in grappling exchanges, utilizing immense natural strength to defend takedowns and maintain dominant positions when the fight hits the canvas. In recent years, he has demonstrated a more calculated version of his "Demolidor" persona, narrowing his focus toward efficiency while still retaining the "one-shot" knockout power that can end a fight in an instant. While his output can sometimes wane in the later stages of a grueling contest, his ability to absorb punishment and recover quickly ensures he is never truly out of a fight until the final bell. After a mixed campaign in 2025 where he faced challenges against high-level pressure grapplers, his recent rapid-fire knockout victory in early 2026 served as a stern reminder to the division that his explosiveness and finishing instincts remain as dangerous as ever, keeping him firmly positioned as a volatile threat to anyone in the top fifteen.

Christian Leroy Duncan is widely considered one of the most creative and visually dynamic strikers currently competing in the middleweight division. He carries a calm and almost detached personality, moving with a rhythmic grace that suggests he is viewing the fight as a complex dance rather than a brawl. His offensive system is built on a high-level foundation of versatile kickboxing, where he utilizes his massive reach and switch-hitting capabilities to launch attacks from angles that most opponents simply cannot anticipate. While he is famous for his highlight-reel spinning elbows and knees, he is statistically a punch-heavy fighter who uses his hands to set up his more exotic maneuvers, often targeting the body with clinical precision to drain his opponent's gas tank. Defensively, he relies on elite footwork and head movement to stay on the perimeter, though he has shown significant improvement in his ability to scramble and defend takedowns against high-level wrestlers. His recent career trajectory has been stellar, punctuated by a masterclass performance in March 2026 against a rugged veteran where he demonstrated that his tactical intelligence has finally caught up to his natural athleticism. By systematically dismantling a top-tier grinder, Duncan proved he is no longer just a "prospect" but a legitimate threat to the top ten, combining a high finishing instinct with the composure to win long, technical battles. As he continues his climb, he remains the premier representative of the "new wave" of British MMA, blending traditional striking arts with a modern, unpredictable flow that makes him a nightmare to prepare for.

fight-history: W vs Azamat Murzakanov (KO/TKO) R3 1:23 - UFC 327

description: Paulo Costa is a polarizing and high-profile figure in the middleweight landscape, possessing a larger-than-life personality that has made him a social media sensation as much as a feared competitor. He approaches his career with a mix of supreme confidence and a cheeky, often unpredictable public persona, but once the cage door closes, he transforms into an incredibly physical and intimidating presence. His offensive game is defined by a heavy-handed approach to boxing where he relentlessly stalks his opponents, looking to trap them against the fence to unleash a staggering volume of power strikes. While he is capable of ending a fight with a single head-on collision, his true specialty lies in his devastating body-work, often using thudding middle kicks and hooks to the liver to systematically break down his opponent's resolve. Despite holding a high-level rank in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, he primarily uses his grappling skills defensively, relying on his massive frame and natural strength to stay upright and maintain the high-paced striking exchanges he favors. Throughout 2024 and 2025, he faced a grueling schedule against the absolute elite of the division, showing incredible durability in wars against former champions while proving that his punching power remains a threat to anyone in the top tier. His recent victory in July 2025 served as a crucial turning point, silencing critics of his activity levels and re-establishing him as a top-fifteen mainstay. As he moves into 2026, he continues to be a dangerous gatekeeper to the title picture, possessing the unique ability to turn any technical matchup into a chaotic and punishing brawl that tests the chin and heart of every man he faces.

Joe Pyfer has rapidly emerged as one of the most compelling and powerful figures in the middleweight division, carrying a gritty, no-nonsense personality forged through a difficult upbringing that has resonated deeply with the MMA community. He approaches his craft with a blue-collar intensity and a clear sense of purpose, often speaking about the mental toughness required to overcome adversity both inside and outside the Octagon. His combat style is centered on a high-pressure striking game where he utilizes a punch-heavy attack to capitalize on his world-class natural power, particularly a straight right hand that can alter the course of a fight instantly. While his primary path to victory often involves hunting for a knockout, he has cultivated a well-rounded grappling foundation that allows him to remain competitive in all areas, showing a dangerous ability to find submissions during scrambles or use his wrestling to control the tempo of a bout. Throughout 2025, he demonstrated significant growth by moving past his early career setbacks, securing dominant wins over established veterans and proving that his finishing instincts are matched by a disciplined tactical approach. His recent performances have showcased an improved ability to manage his output across multiple rounds, ensuring he remains a constant threat even as the fight progresses into the later stages. As he prepares for the biggest stage of his career in the coming days, Pyfer stands on the precipice of true stardom, representing a blend of raw physical strength and the relentless work ethic of a fighter who has truly earned his place among the world's elite.

Roman Dolidze is a physically imposing and stoic competitor who has carved out a reputation as one of the most dangerous specialists in the middleweight division. Hailing from Georgia, he carries a quiet but intense demeanor, often appearing completely unfazed by the high-pressure environment of the Octagon. His combat system is built around a punishing clinch game and a sophisticated grappling attack that makes him a nightmare for anyone who allows the fight to enter close quarters. While he utilizes a fundamental boxing approach to close the distance, his true effectiveness is found when he can manipulate his opponent's posture to land heavy short-range strikes or initiate transitions to the canvas. Once on the ground, he possesses a rare ability to hunt for high-level submissions, particularly from his back or during complex scrambles, while simultaneously delivering heavy impact through his top-control. Throughout late 2025 and early 2026, he has served as a formidable gatekeeper to the top ten, testing the defensive layers of the division’s rising stars with his immense natural strength and durable chin. Although he has faced tactical difficulties against high-volume strikers who prefer to stay on the outside, his victory over established top-tier names has proven that he remains a constant threat capable of finishing the fight at any moment. As he moves forward into the 2026 season, Dolidze remains a critical puzzle for the division, representing a unique blend of old-school physical grinding and modern, technical submission grappling that requires a perfect performance to overcome.

Alex Pereira is a generational talent whose career has become a living legend, defined by an aura of stoic, ancestral power and a terrifying ability to end any fight with a single connection. He carries a deeply reserved and focused personality, often appearing almost statuesque during face-offs, which only amplifies the psychological pressure he exerts on his opponents. His fighting style is a masterclass in modern kickboxing, where he utilizes a deceptive, low-volume approach to set traps for his signature "touch of death" left hook and bone-shattering calf kicks. Despite coming from a pure striking background, he has developed an elite defensive wrestling system under the tutelage of Glover Teixeira, allowing him to keep his fights standing where his technical superiority and extreme power can dictate the outcome. In 2025, his rivalry with Magomed Ankalaev defined the light heavyweight division; after dropping a tactical decision in March, Pereira returned in October at UFC 320 to reclaim his throne with a spectacular first-round knockout that silenced all doubters. Following this redemption, he made the shocking decision in early 2026 to relinquish his light heavyweight belt to move up to heavyweight, eyeing a historic opportunity to become the first three-division champion in the history of the UFC. He is currently scheduled to face Ciryl Gane for the interim heavyweight title on June 14, 2026, at the unprecedented "UFC White House" event, a move that has solidified his status as the most ambitious and fearless fighter of the modern era.

Magomed Ankalaev is widely regarded as the most technically complete and tactically disciplined fighter in the light heavyweight division today. He possesses a stoic and professional personality, characterized by a quiet confidence that reflects his deep roots in the Dagestani combat sports system. His fighting style is a highly efficient masterclass in balanced mixed martial arts, where he utilizes a sophisticated southpaw striking game to dictate the distance and pace of his bouts. While he is capable of delivering fight-ending power through his clinical counter-striking and heavy head kicks, his primary strength lies in his world-class defensive layers and high fight intelligence. He is nearly impossible to take down, and once he initiates his own grappling, he employs a heavy, sambo-based pressure that can stifle even the most explosive opponents. Throughout 2025, his career reached new heights as he finally captured UFC gold by outpointing the legendary Alex Pereira in their first encounter, showcasing a level of tactical perfection that few can match. However, his momentum was briefly halted in October 2025 when he suffered a rapid TKO loss in their immediate rematch, a setback that has only fueled his drive to reclaim the throne. As of early 2026, Ankalaev sits at the top of the rankings as the clear number one contender, recently confirming his return for a major headlining bout in Abu Dhabi this July. Whether he is methodically dismantling elite strikers on the feet or neutralizing power-hitters with his grappling, he remains the division's ultimate technical puzzle and the odds-on favorite to lead the post-Pereira era of the 205-pound division

fight-history: L vs Carlos Ulberg (KO/TKO) R1 3:45 - UFC 327

description: Jiří Procházka is one of the most enigmatic and captivating figures in modern mixed martial arts, living by a strict code of the "Modern Samurai" inspired by the teachings of the Book of Five Rings. He possesses a deeply spiritual and intense personality, often isolating himself in total darkness for days at a time to sharpen his mental focus and confront his inner demons before a bout. His fighting style is a chaotic and beautiful manifestation of "BJP"—his personal brand of "Bomby Jak Pča" (Bombs Like Fck)—which relies on a wildly unorthodox striking game that defies traditional stance and guard. He operates with a high volume of creative strikes, utilizing a diverse punching arsenal that accounts for nearly three-quarters of his offense, while constantly threatening with flying knees and spinning elbows that can materialize from any angle. While his defensive metrics are often low due to his hands-down approach, he possesses a legendary ability to recover from significant damage, frequently resetting his nervous system mid-fight to find a spectacular finish. His grappling is equally unpredictable, focusing on frantic scrambles and a high-level submission threat that famously earned him the title against Glover Teixeira. Following a dominant 2025 where he dispatched both Jamahal Hill and Khalil Rountree Jr. via knockout, he has solidified his position as the top challenger in the world. He is currently scheduled to headline UFC 327 in Miami on April 11, 2026, where he will face Carlos Ulberg for the vacant light heavyweight championship, seeking to reclaim the throne and prove that his "Bushido" spirit is the ultimate force in the division.

fight-history: W vs Jiri Prochazka (KO/TKO) R1 3:45 - UFC 327

description: Carlos Ulberg has evolved into the most polished and dangerous striking specialist currently on the rise in the light heavyweight division, representing the elite standard of New Zealand’s City Kickboxing. He carries a composed and highly professional personality, characterized by a quiet confidence and a focus on technical perfection that often leaves his opponents looking several steps behind. His combat style is a masterclass in elite-level kickboxing, where he utilizes a piercing jab and exceptional footwork to set up a devastating check hook and straight right hand. While he possesses the raw athleticism and speed to engage in rapid-fire exchanges, he has developed a remarkably patient approach, preferring to dismantle his opponents from the outside before identifying the perfect moment to deploy his fight-ending power. Defensively, he has shown significant growth since his lone career setback, implementing a high-level takedown defense and improved head movement that allows him to remain safe while operating at a high offensive output. Throughout 2025, his stock reached an all-time high as he secured a series of dominant victories over former champions and top-tier contenders, punctuated by a clinical first-round stoppage of Dominick Reyes in September. As he approaches his first championship opportunity at UFC 327 in April 2026, he stands on the verge of greatness, combining his world-class kickboxing pedigree with a newfound tactical maturity that makes him a terrifying puzzle for anyone in the weight class.
