Khalil Rountree Jr. is one of the most remarkable stories of personal and athletic transformation in the UFC, evolving from a struggling youth into a premier, world-class striking specialist. He possesses an introspective and deeply authentic personality, often speaking openly about his journey with mental health and his philosophical approach to the "art of eight limbs." His combat style is a violent and highly refined expression of traditional Muay Thai, characterized by a southpaw stance that serves as a platform for some of the most specialized and damaging leg-work in the sport. While he has the technical depth to engage in high-speed kickboxing matches, he primarily relies on a punch-heavy attack to set up his fight-altering power, specifically utilizing a piston-like straight left hand and oblique kicks that can compromise an opponent's structural integrity in seconds. Defensively, he has made massive strides in his ability to remain calm under fire, preferring to stay in the pocket where his devastating clinch knees and elbows can punish anyone attempting to initiate grappling. His 2025 campaign was a rollercoaster of elite competition; he secured a career-defining unanimous decision over former champion Jamahal Hill in June, demonstrating a new level of tactical patience and five-round endurance. Although he recently came up short in a "Fight of the Night" war against Jiří Procházka in October 2025, his stock has never been higher as a premier action fighter who possesses the rare "X-factor" to knockout any man in the division. Entering 2026, he remains firmly entrenched in the top five, serving as a lethal gatekeeper to the title picture and a fan favorite who consistently delivers high-stakes, highlight-reel drama.

Jan Błachowicz remains one of the most respected and physically imposing veterans in the light heavyweight division, carrying a persona defined by the "Legendary Polish Power" and a calm, approachable maturity outside the cage. He possesses a stoic and humble personality, often seen as a statesman of the sport who relies on a deep well of experience and tactical patience to navigate high-stakes battles. His fighting style is built upon a foundation of fundamental kickboxing and a crushing punching game, where he utilizes a thudding left hook and clinical straight rights to punish opponents who miscalculate the distance. While he is famous for his ability to end a fight with a single heavy connection, he also employs a sophisticated body-kicking game that he uses to sap the energy of his foes over the course of five rounds. On the ground, he is a seasoned specialist who excels in using his massive frame to maintain dominant top control, frequently looking for submissions or using heavy ground-and-pound to dictate the terms of the engagement. His recent campaign in 2025 was a testament to his durability, as he returned from a serious shoulder surgery to face the division's new elite, battling through a grueling draw against the heavy-hitting Bogdan Guskov in December. Despite being one of the older athletes in the top five, his ability to absorb punishment and stay technically sound in the face of adversity ensures he remains a premier hurdle for any title hopeful. As he prepares for a highly anticipated rematch with Guskov on May 9, 2026, Błachowicz continues to prove that his combination of physical strength and championship-level fight IQ makes him a permanent threat in the 205-pound title picture.

fight-history: L vs Paulo Costa (KO/TKO) R3 1:23 - UFC 327

description: Azamat Murzakanov is the dark horse of the light heavyweight division, carrying an undefeated record and a reputation for cold, clinical efficiency that perfectly matches his nickname. His personality is understated and intensely focused, reflecting a background in elite hand-to-hand combat and military service where discipline is paramount. His fighting style is built around a deceptively fast and explosive southpaw striking game, where he utilizes superior timing and a "short" punching style to catch opponents coming in. Despite being one of the shorter fighters in the weight class, he possesses a rare level of natural power in his hands, specifically a lead hook and a straight left that have consistently put elite competition to sleep. While his striking is his primary weapon, he maintains an exceptionally high takedown defense and a solid grappling base that allows him to dictate where the fight takes place, often choosing to keep the match standing to exploit his technical advantages. Throughout 2025, he surged through the rankings with a series of devastating first-round finishes, most notably a clinical dismantling of Aleksandar Rakić in October that proved he belongs in the division's upper echelon. As he heads toward a major showdown with Paulo Costa at UFC 327 in April 2026, "The Professional" stands as a terrifying technical hurdle for the elite, combining the patience of a veteran with the explosive finishing instincts of a man who has never tasted defeat.

Jamahal Hill is a supreme natural talent who relies on a high-level boxing pedigree and a deep-seated belief in his own punching power to navigate the light heavyweight elite. He carries a vocal and highly confident personality, often engaging in direct psychological warfare with his peers and critics alike, driven by a desire to prove he is the most dangerous "pure" striker in the division. His fighting style is that of a clinical technical boxer who utilizes a long, snapping southpaw jab and a deceptive straight left to find the chin of his opponents with terrifying accuracy. While he possesses the range to kick, he is heavily punch-dominant, preferring to use his hands to create openings for the one-shot knockouts that have become his trademark. Defensively, he operates as a specialist who focuses on keeping the fight standing at all costs, utilizing his long frame and spatial awareness to force his opponents into the striking exchanges where he holds the advantage. After a historic run to the title in 2023, his recent 2025 campaign proved difficult as he faced the division's most explosive power-hitters, suffering high-stakes setbacks that have forced him to recalibrate his defensive approach. Despite these recent losses, his ability to out-duel legends like Glover Teixeira and starch top contenders ensures he remains a premier threat to the crown. As he prepares for a crucial bounce-back performance in mid-2026, Hill remains one of the most dangerous individuals at 205 pounds, possessing the rare "touch of sleep" in both hands that can turn any technical battle into a short night for his opposition.

Bogdan Guskov is a terrifying offensive juggernaut who has rapidly ascended the light heavyweight rankings through a series of violent, highlight-reel finishes. He possesses a calm but predatory personality, often characterized by a singular focus on the knockout that has earned him a reputation as one of the most dangerous first-round threats in the division. His fighting style is that of a high-intensity pressure striker who relies on a devastating punching arsenal to overwhelm his opponents from the opening bell. While he is a capable kickboxer, he is heavily punch-dominant, utilizing a thudding straight right and a lead hook that carry genuine one-shot power. On the ground, he maintains a solid all-round grappling base, though he primarily uses his wrestling defensively to ensure the fight remains in the pocket where he can dictate the pace with his hands. Throughout 2025, Guskov became a breakout star, punctuating a four-fight knockout streak with a clinical first-round stoppage of Nikita Krylov in July. His momentum led to a high-stakes encounter with former champion Jan Błachowicz at UFC 323 in December 2025, a grueling three-round war that ultimately ended in a majority draw after Guskov dropped the legend in the second round but struggled with the veteran's leg kicks late in the fight. As of March 2026, he is preparing for the most significant moment of his career: a highly anticipated rematch with Błachowicz scheduled for UFC 328 on May 9, 2026. With a 100% finish rate in all of his professional victories, "The Hunger" remains the division's ultimate "kill-or-be-killed" contender, looking to secure a definitive win over a legend to punch his ticket to a top-five ranking.

Robert Whittaker is a paragon of technical excellence and martial arts discipline, entering the light heavyweight division as one of the most decorated champions in UFC history. Known for his "nice guy" persona and unmatched professionalism, he carries a competitive fire that has allowed him to remain at the elite level for over a decade. His approach is built on a foundation of lightning-fast blitzes and a snapping jab that is arguably the best in the sport. He utilizes a karate-based stance to enter and exit the pocket before his opponents can react, often hiding a devastating high kick behind his punching combinations. Defensively, he possesses a nearly impenetrable takedown defense that has frustrated the most elite wrestlers in the world. His transition to 205 lbs in early 2026 was a strategic move focused on career longevity and maximizing his performance without the draining weight cuts that saw him lose a razor-thin split decision to Reinier de Ridder in July 2025. Now walking around at nearly 108 kg, Whittaker has packed on functional mass to handle the physicality of larger opponents while maintaining his trademark speed advantage. As he prepares for a high-stakes debut in July 2026—potentially against Magomed Ankalaev in Abu Dhabi—he remains a tactical genius who relies on his world-class fight IQ and legendary "Bobby Knuckles" grit to prove that skill and speed can conquer raw size in the light heavyweight division.

Dominick Reyes is a resilient and high-level athlete who has experienced both the pinnacle of title contention and the harshest depths of the sport’s volatility. He carries a serious and determined personality, often fueled by the chip on his shoulder stemming from his razor-thin 2020 decision loss to Jon Jones—a fight many still believe he won. His approach is defined by elite southpaw boxing, where he utilizes a long, straight left hand and devastating body kicks to control the rhythm of the fight from the outside. He is at his most dangerous when he can find his range early, using his "Devastator" power to capitalize on small defensive lapses. While he has struggled with durability in the past against the division's elite power-hitters, he remains one of the best defensive wrestlers at 205 lbs, possessing an uncanny ability to stay upright and force opponents into technical striking matches. After a difficult multi-year slump, Reyes underwent a significant career resurgence throughout 2024 and early 2025, stringing together three consecutive knockout victories that proved his speed and timing were still world-class. Although he suffered a setback in his most recent headlining appearance against the surging Carlos Ulberg in September 2025, his veteran savvy and ability to end a fight in an instant keep him firmly inside the top ten. As he looks toward his next challenge at UFC 327 in April 2026 against Johnny Walker, Reyes remains a vital fixture of the division, representing a high-level bridge between the established guard and the new wave of light heavyweight talent.

Aleksandar Rakić is a highly disciplined and physically gifted athlete who has long been considered one of the most dangerous technical threats in the light heavyweight division. He carries a focused and intensely competitive personality, often approaching his fights with a methodical, almost scientific mindset that prioritizes structural damage and distance control. His approach is built upon a high-level kickboxing foundation where he utilizes his significant height and reach to dominate the perimeter, most notably through a devastating array of calf and leg kicks that can compromise an opponent’s mobility in the early rounds. He is a specialist at maintaining a safe distance, using a long jab and heavy straight rights to punish anyone attempting to bridge the gap. Defensively, he is one of the premier anti-grapplers in the weight class, possessing an elite takedown defense that forces his opponents to engage in the technical striking battles he prefers. Despite his clear technical skill, Rakić has faced a string of high-stakes adversity over the last two years, including a serious knee injury and a run of losses against the division's absolute elite. His most recent appearance in October 2025 saw him fall to the undefeated Azamat Murzakanov, marking a difficult four-fight skid that has forced him to reassess his path to the title. As he looks to snap this streak in 2026, he remains a formidable "Rocket" who possesses the physical tools and tactical intelligence to beat any man in the top ten if he can successfully blend his power with a more durable defensive shell.

Johnny Walker is one of the most eccentric and physically gifted athletes in the history of the light heavyweight division, known for a high-energy personality that makes him a perennial fan favorite. He approaches the sport with a sense of joy and unpredictability, often dancing his way to the Octagon, though this playfulness belies a terrifying level of explosive power. His combat system is characterized by an unorthodox and flashy striking game where he utilizes his massive 82-inch reach to launch flying knees, spinning backfists, and devastating elbows from positions that defy traditional mechanics. While he has moved toward a more measured approach under the tutelage of John Kavanagh at SBG Ireland, he remains a "glass cannon" capable of starching elite competition in seconds or falling victim to a single well-placed counter. His grappling has seen steady improvement, particularly his ability to utilize his long levers for submissions and scrambles, though he primarily uses these skills to keep the fight standing. After a difficult stretch in 2024 against top contenders, Walker successfully rebuilt his momentum in August 2025 with a brutal second-round TKO of Zhang Mingyang in Shanghai, earning a Performance of the Night bonus. As he prepares for a high-stakes clash against Dominick Reyes at UFC 327 on April 11, 2026, Walker remains the division’s ultimate wild card—a man who can end any fight in a heartbeat with a strike no one sees coming.

Nikita Krylov is a seasoned and highly versatile veteran who has long been one of the most prolific finishers in the light heavyweight division. He carries a stoic and battle-hardened personality, reflecting a career spanning over 40 professional bouts and a reputation for never being in a boring fight. His approach is defined by a high-paced and aggressive striking game that utilizes heavy kicks and clinical punching combinations to overwhelm his opponents from the opening bell. He is particularly dangerous in the first round, where his finishing instincts are at their sharpest, often finding unique openings for head kicks or rapid-fire flurries. On the ground, he is a sophisticated submission threat who excels in capitalizing on chaotic scrambles, possessing a wide array of chokes and joint locks that make him a constant danger even when working from his back. Throughout 2025, Krylov faced significant adversity, suffering consecutive knockout losses to elite power-hitters that raised questions about his long-term durability. However, he silenced doubters in January 2026 with a dramatic "buzzer-beater" knockout of Modestas Bukauskas, demonstrating that his veteran savvy and ability to maintain a high output into the later rounds remain intact. As he moves deeper into 2026, "The Miner" continues to serve as a formidable test for any aspiring contender, offering a relentless pace and a "kill-or-be-killed" mentality that requires total focus to overcome.

Dustin Jacoby is a high-level striking specialist and former Glory Kickboxing title challenger who has successfully translated his world-class stand-up pedigree into a long and respected career within the UFC. He carries a professional and remarkably resilient personality, often characterized by a "workman-like" approach to the sport that has seen him navigate multiple stints in the promotion. His combat system is a masterclass in technical kickboxing, where he utilizes a highly efficient jab and exceptional feints to set up a diverse array of leg kicks and crisp punching combinations. He is at his most dangerous when he can establish a rhythmic, high-volume pace from the outside, picking apart opponents with surgical precision rather than relying on pure individual power. Defensively, he relies on his veteran experience and disciplined footwork to stay clear of high-danger zones, while maintaining a solid takedown defense that forces his opponents to play his game on the feet. After a difficult stretch in late 2023 and early 2024, Jacoby has undergone a late-career renaissance, entering March 2026 on a three-fight knockout streak. His most recent performance in February 2026 was a clinical second-round stoppage of the surging prospect Julius Walker, a win that propelled him back into the top 15. As one of the most technically sound strikers in the division, Jacoby remains a difficult tactical puzzle for the next generation of light heavyweights, proving that his "Hanyak" spirit and elite timing are as sharp as ever as he continues his pursuit of a top-ten ranking.

fight-history: L vs Alonzo Menifield (KO/TKO - Punches) R1 4:15 - UFC Fight Night: Song vs. Figueiredo

description: Zhang Mingyang is a breakout sensation from China who has introduced a frantic and lethal energy to the light heavyweight landscape. He carries a humble but fiercely determined personality, representing the rising tide of Chinese talent in the heavier weight classes with a "Mountain Tiger" intensity that matches his moniker. His approach is defined by a relentless, high-pressure striking game that aims to end fights as quickly as possible; remarkably, every single one of his 19 professional victories has occurred in the first round. He possesses terrifying power in his hands, particularly a straight right and lead hook that can turn out the lights of even the most durable veterans. While he is primarily known for his striking, he maintains a surprisingly versatile grappling game rooted in Sanda, which he uses to maintain an aggressive pace and punish opponents who try to take him down. His 2025 campaign saw him reach new heights, including a clinical first-round stoppage of former title challenger Anthony Smith that signaled his arrival as a true top-15 threat. Although he suffered his first UFC setback in a chaotic main event against Johnny Walker in August 2025, his stock remains high as a premier action fighter. As of March 2026, Zhang is set to return to action at UFC Macau on May 30, 2026, where he will face the heavy-hitting Alonzo Menifield in a bout that promises to be an absolute explosion of power.

Tom Aspinall represents the peak of heavyweight evolution in the post-Jon Jones era, blending the nimble footwork and lightning-fast hand speed of a middleweight with the devastating one-punch knockout power inherent to his 260-pound frame. An elite amateur boxer before transitioning to MMA, his striking is characterized by a piston-like jab and exceptional lateral movement that allows him to exit exchanges before opponents can counter. Beyond his striking, Aspinall is a high-level Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu black belt capable of snatching submissions the moment a fight hits the canvas, evidenced by his dominant grappling displays against world-class opposition. His tactical versatility is managed by a superior Fight IQ, enabling him to identify and exploit defensive lapses with clinical efficiency. Despite a high-profile "No Contest" due to an accidental foul in late 2025 and a previous freak injury loss, his record for the shortest average fight time in UFC history underscores a predatory instinct that makes him the most dangerous finisher in the division's history.

Ciryl "Bon Gamin" Gane is arguably the most technically proficient "pure" striker to ever compete in the UFC's Heavyweight division. Transitioning from an undefeated Muay Thai career, Gane utilizes a unique "point-fighting" style that relies on elite footwork, constant stance-switching, and a sophisticated long-range kicking game. Unlike traditional heavyweights who look for the singular knockout blow, Gane systematically dismantles opponents with a "legal eye-poke" jab and punishing low-line side kicks to the lead knee. His agility allows him to maintain a high-volume output while remaining one of the least-hit fighters in the category. While his wrestling and bottom-game have historically been identified as his primary weaknesses—most notably in his losses to Francis Ngannou and Jon Jones—his clinch work and defensive movement have improved significantly by 2026. Following a controversial No Contest against Tom Aspinall in late 2025 where he demonstrated a significant speed advantage early on, Gane remains the divisional gatekeeper to the pound-for-pound elite, forcing every opponent to solve a complex movement puzzle or be outclassed over five rounds.

Standing at a towering 6'7", Alexander "Drago" Volkov is the premier rangy striker of the heavyweight division. His game is built on a world-class "stiff" jab and a front-teep kick that effectively creates a no-man's-land for shorter opponents. Volkov has significantly evolved in his veteran years, moving away from a high-volume "patter" style toward a more calculated, punishing approach that utilizes his massive frame to frame-off and land devastating knees in the clinch. His defensive grappling, once considered a liability, has become a fortress; he successfully neutralized the relentless wrestling of Jailton Almeida in late 2025 by using his height to create leverage and maintain distance. While he lacks the explosive "kill-shot" power of someone like Pavlovich, his technical attrition and cardiovascular efficiency allow him to win deep into five-round fights. Now in his late 30s, Volkov relies on a superior Fight IQ to out-think his younger peers, making him a perpetual title threat who is rarely outclassed on the feet.

fight-history: W vs Tallison Teixeira (KO/TKO - Punches) R1 0:39 - UFC Fight Night: Song vs. Figueiredo

description: Sergei Pavlovich remains the most feared "pure" power hitter in the heavyweight division, possessing a freakish 84-inch reach that allows him to land fight-ending blows from distances where his opponents feel safe. His game is built entirely around his "white-collar" boxing—a terrifying combination of high-volume pressure and nuclear force in both hands. After a record-breaking streak of six consecutive first-round knockouts, Pavlovich faced a reality check in 2024 with losses to Tom Aspinall and Alexander Volkov, which exposed a need for more tactical patience. By 2026, he has shown significant maturation in his approach, evidenced by his 2025 wins over Jairzinho Rozenstruik and Waldo Cortes-Acosta, where he proved he could maintain his threat over a full fifteen minutes rather than just hunting for the early exit. While he rarely utilizes his Greco-Roman wrestling offensively, his defensive wrestling remains elite, serving primarily to keep the fight standing where he can maximize his reach advantage. Pavlovich is the ultimate divisional litmus test: if you cannot survive the first three minutes of his blizzard-like punching combinations, you simply do not belong at the top of the weight class.

fight-history: L vs Josh Hokit (U-Dec) R3 5:00 - UFC 327

description: Curtis "Razor" Blaydes remains the premier wrestling specialist of the heavyweight division. Holding the record for the most takedowns in the weight class, his game is built on a relentless double-leg entry and a soul-crushing top-heavy pressure that transitions into brutal Ground and Pound. While his Technical Boxing has improved significantly over the years—moving away from a pure wrestling approach to a more balanced style that uses his overhand right to set up level changes—he is still most effective when he is suffocating opponents against the cage. After a tough knockout loss in mid-2025, Blaydes bounced back in early 2026 by dominating a high-ranked contender over three rounds, proving that his wrestling is still the ultimate "black hole" for anyone without elite takedown defense. He remains the most difficult tactical matchup for the division's strikers, acting as the final hurdle for anyone seeking a title shot against Tom Aspinall.

Waldo Cortes-Acosta has evolved into the division's most conditioned "Big Man." Utilizing a Pressure Striker style rooted in his professional boxing background, Waldo maintains a pace that most heavyweights cannot match over three rounds. His game revolves around a snapping, piston-like jab and a high-frequency 1-2 combination. While he was previously criticized for a "safety-first" approach, his 2025 run—including back-to-back first-round knockouts of Ante Delija and Shamil Gaziev—proved he has found the "kill switch" in his hands. His Defensive Grappling has become his greatest technical asset; he uses excellent lateral movement and a wide base to keep the fight standing, forcing wrestlers into exhausting striking battles. With a "chin of granite" and a gas tank that looks more like a Middleweight's, Waldo is a nightmare for older veterans who rely on early finishes.

Jailton "Malhadinho" Almeida is the most specialized grappler the heavyweight division has seen in decades. Operating as a pure Submission Specialist, his game plan is binary: close the distance immediately, initiate a blast double-leg or body lock, and transition to the back. Unlike the traditional "Ground and Pound" heavyweights, Almeida prioritizes positional dominance and "wet blanket" control to find rear-naked chokes. His 2025 campaign was a rollercoaster; after being neutralized by the technical sprawl of Alexander Volkov in a frustrating decision loss, he bounced back by submitting Marcin Tybura in under three minutes. While his striking remains rudimentary—functioning almost entirely as a vehicle to get into the clinch—his speed and athleticism allow him to out-scramble much larger men. He remains the division's ultimate "specialist" hurdle, a fighter who can make elite contenders look like amateurs if they cannot stop the initial entry.

Serghei "The Polar Bear" Spivac is the division's premier chain-wrestler. Unlike the explosive blast-doubles of other heavyweights, Spivac relies on a Judo and Sambo base, utilizing trips, tosses, and body locks from the clinch. His game is built on "wearing" his opponents down; once he gets a fight to the mat, his ground-and-pound is methodical, and his submission game—specifically his arm-triangle and armbar—is elite. 2025 was a tough year for the Moldovan, suffering a knockout loss to Jailton Almeida and a frustrating decision loss to Waldo Cortes-Acosta. However, he started 2026 with a dominant grappling display against Ante Delija in February, reminding the division that if you can't stop his clinch, you're in for a very long night. His striking is functional but primarily serves as a smoke-screen to close the distance.

Rizvan Kuniev is the "dark horse" of the top 10. Emerging from the legendary Gorets Fight Club, he brings a more polished striking game than many of his Dagestani peers, often functioning as a Pressure Striker who uses heavy knees and short hooks in the pocket. However, his foundation remains his All-Round Grappling; he possesses an elite 93% takedown defense rate, which he used to neutralize Jailton Almeida's wrestling in their February 2026 clash. Kuniev's only major UFC setback was a razor-thin split decision loss to Curtis Blaydes in 2025—a fight many fans felt he won. He is a "grinder" with finishing power, specializing in breaking opponents against the fence before finishing with ground-and-pound or a opportunistic guillotine.

Derrick Lewis remains the ultimate "touch of death" in the heavyweight division. Despite being 41, his Counter Striker style is as dangerous as ever; he lulls opponents into a false sense of security before exploding with a single right hand. While his technical wrestling is statistically low, his "just stand up" philosophy—an incredible ability to explode out of bottom position—makes him a nightmare for grapplers to hold down. After a 35-second destruction of Tallison Teixeira in 2025, he most recently fell to Waldo Cortes-Acosta in January 2026, proving that high-volume strikers can still out-point him if they avoid the big shot.

Marcin Tybura operates as the division's most seasoned tactical veteran. He excels at neutralizing more explosive opponents by forcing them into a high-paced clinch game and using his weight to drain their energy against the fence. His offensive approach relies on consistent volume rather than one-punch power, using a steady stream of kicks and straight punches to set up his entries. Once he establishes top position, he is methodical with his weight distribution, making it difficult for opponents to find the space required to stand back up.

Ante Delija brings the aggressive, power-heavy lineage of the Cro Cop Team into the top 10. He is a physically imposing athlete who looks to dictate the pace early with heavy hands and a relentless forward march. While he possesses significant knockout power in his right hand, he often uses his striking to crash into a clinch where he can transition into powerful takedowns. Once on the mat, he focuses on heavy impact, looking to finish fights through aggressive strikes from a dominant top position rather than hunting for technical submissions.

Mick Parkin is a highly mobile heavyweight who relies on superior cardiovascular conditioning and consistent striking output. He utilizes a sharp, educated jab to control distance and disrupt the rhythm of his opponents. While he is comfortable working through grappling exchanges and maintaining top position, his primary focus is on high-volume boxing combinations. He shows a high level of technical discipline, rarely over-committing to power shots and instead opting to wear down his opposition over the course of the fight.

Alexandr Romanov is a powerhouse wrestler who looks to impose his physical will through explosive takedowns and heavy pressure. He specializes in high-amplitude slams and upper-body tosses, quickly transitioning from the clinch to dominant positions on the mat. Once he has secured a lead, he utilizes a smothering style of top control combined with heavy, short-range strikes to force openings for submissions. While he possesses heavy hands in the pocket, his striking primarily serves as a means to force his opponents into a grappling match where he holds a significant technical advantage.

Shamil Gaziev is a physically imposing heavyweight who relies on a high-pressure approach to overwhelm opponents in the early stages of a fight. He utilizes his massive frame to crowd the pocket, often looking for a heavy uppercut or short-range hooks to force a stoppage. While he possesses a strong foundation in wrestling and sambo, he primarily uses his grappling skills to maintain the fight on the feet or to pin opponents against the cage where he can land knees and short strikes. Despite showing significant finishing power in the first round, his primary challenge has been maintaining that same intensity into the later rounds of the fight.

Valter Walker stands as one of the most physically imposing figures in the division, utilizing his massive 6'6" frame to dictate where the fight takes place. Unlike his brother Johnny, Valter avoids high-risk chaos in favor of a heavy, suffocating wrestling game. He excels at using his long reach to initiate ties, where he then employs high-amplitude takedowns or grueling cage-presses to exhaust his opponents. On the ground, he is a specialist at maintaining heavy top-side control, using short-range strikes to break down a defender's guard. His game is built on durability and a relentless pace that often sees him pull away in the second and third rounds as his opponents tire under his weight.

personal-info.name: Tallison Teixeira
personal-info.fighting-out-of: São José do Rio Preto, São Paulo

fight-history: L vs Sergei Pavlovich (KO/TKO - Punches) R1 0:39 - UFC Fight Night: Song vs. Figueiredo

description: Tallison Teixeira is one of the most physically imposing prospects in UFC Heavyweight history, standing 6'7" with a freakish 83" reach. A natural finisher whose entire career has been built on first-round stoppages, his power is elite — evidenced by his 78% finishing rate and a stunning TKO of Derrick Lewis in his UFC debut. However, his two UFC losses have exposed serious defensive vulnerabilities: he was finished in under 40 seconds on both occasions, by Lewis and Pavlovich, suggesting his chin and fight IQ under elite pressure are genuine question marks. His BJJ black belt — awarded by his older brother, who also coaches him — gives him a credible submission threat, and his Tuivasa win showed he can grind out a three-round decision when needed. At just 26 with a tiny UFC sample size, Teixeira remains one of the division's highest-ceiling prospects, but whether his defence can catch up to his physical gifts remains the defining question of his career.

Vitor Petrino is a converted light heavyweight who has brought elite athleticism and explosive power into the heavyweight top 15. He is a chaotic and physically gifted competitor who thrives in high-paced exchanges where he can land his signature flying knees or looping power shots. While he began his career primarily as a striker, he has developed a versatile grappling game, often using explosive suplexes and heavy top pressure to secure rounds. His transition to heavyweight in 2025 has seen him successfully utilize his speed advantage against larger, slower opponents. However, his high-energy style often leads to significant cardiovascular decline in the later rounds, forcing him to rely on his grit and recovery to see out decisions if he cannot find an early finish.

Mackenzie Dern reached her lifelong goal by capturing the vacant Strawweight title in October 2025 with a dominant performance against Virna Jandiroba. Her evolution has been marked by a significant improvement in her standing pressure, where she uses heavy looping shots to force opponents into the clinch. However, her game remains centered on her world-class Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu pedigree. She possesses the most dangerous ground game in the division; once she initiates a scramble or secures a takedown, her ability to find an armbar or rear-naked choke from any position is unmatched. She has successfully transitioned from a pure grappler into a fighter who weaponizes her striking to create the chaos necessary to bring the fight into her world.

Zhang Weili returns to her natural home at 115 lbs after a brief, unsuccessful bid for the Flyweight title against Valentina Shevchenko. Despite that loss, she remains the gold standard for athleticism and technical brutality in the strawweight division. She operates with relentless forward movement, utilizing punishing leg kicks and high-accuracy boxing combinations that overwhelm her opponents' defenses. Her clinch game is arguably the best in the weight class, featuring devastating elbows and knees. While she is primarily known for her striking, her defensive wrestling and ability to scramble back to her feet make her incredibly difficult for grapplers to neutralize.

Tatiana Suarez remains the most feared wrestler in the history of the women's strawweight division. A two-time world bronze medalist in freestyle wrestling, she translates those elite skills into an MMA setting by utilizing a relentless chain-wrestling attack that few can resist. Her game plan is built on absolute positional dominance; she uses her superior strength and leverage to pin opponents to the canvas, where she systematically drains their energy with heavy top pressure. While she suffered her first professional setback in a title bout against Zhang Weili in early 2025, she quickly returned to form with a dominant win over Amanda Lemos later that year. Her striking is primarily defensive and functional, serving as a vehicle to close the distance and initiate the clinch where she is virtually untouchable.

Virna Jandiroba is one of the most technical and persistent submission threats in the strawweight division. A former Invicta FC champion, her game is built entirely around her elite Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu foundation. She utilizes a relentless pressure-based striking style to close distance, though her primary objective is always to initiate grappling exchanges. She is a specialist at finding the back or securing armbars in transitions, possessing some of the highest control-time statistics in the weight class. In October 2025, she came up just short in a five-round title fight against Mackenzie Dern, where her wrestling was highly effective but her striking defense struggled. Despite that loss, she remains a formidable hurdle for any contender, as her ability to dictate the fight on the mat is nearly peerless.

Yan Xiaonan is the division's premier volume striker, utilizing a background in Sanda to maintain a relentless pace. She operates with a primary focus on lateral movement and rapid-fire punching combinations, often overwhelming opponents with sheer activity rather than singular knockout power. Her game is built on a high-frequency jab and side kicks that disrupt the rhythm of her opposition. While her early UFC career was defined by her striking, her move to Team Alpha Male has bolstered her defensive wrestling and ability to navigate high-level grappling exchanges. She remains a difficult out for anyone in the top five due to her durability and a gas tank that allows her to maintain a championship-level tempo for the full duration of a fight.

Gillian Robertson has transformed herself into the ultimate "boogeywoman" of the strawweight division. Holding the record for the most finishes and submissions in women's UFC history, she operates with a single-minded focus on taking the fight to the mat. Her game is characterized by a relentless pursuit of the takedown, often wading through strikes to secure a clinch or a double-leg. Once on the ground, she is a positional shark, using a smothering top-game to force her opponents into giving up their backs or necks. Her 2025 and early 2026 runs have been career-defining, featuring a five-fight win streak that includes a TKO victory over Marina Rodriguez and a dominant tactical decision over former title challenger Amanda Lemos. While her striking is secondary, it has become much more durable, allowing her to survive early onslaughts to eventually drag her opposition into deep, grappling-heavy waters.

Loopy Godinez is the division's most prolific "workhorse," known for her incredible activity and high-frequency fighting schedule. She operates with a crisp, boxing-centric approach on the feet, utilizing rapid-fire combinations and a high-level defensive shell to out-point her opponents. Her game is anchored by elite-level defensive wrestling, which allows her to dictate whether a fight remains standing or transitions to the mat. When she chooses to grapple, she employs a physically strong, top-heavy style that prioritizes positional control and short strikes. Following a difficult 2024 where she faced top-tier contenders like Virna Jandiroba and Mackenzie Dern, she has surged back into title contention in late 2025 and early 2026 with dominant technical performances against Julia Polastri and Jessica Andrade.

Tabatha Ricci is a high-pace competitor who effectively blends her background in Judo and Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu with a rapidly evolving boxing game. She utilizes a constant forward march to disrupt her opponent's rhythm, often using technical combinations to hide her entries into the clinch. Once she establishes a connection, she employs slick trips and tosses to bring the fight to the mat. Her ground game is defined by active positional changes and a constant hunt for submissions, particularly armbars. While she struggled with the elite striking range of Yan Xiaonan in late 2024, she made a major statement in mid-2025 by securing her first UFC knockout victory over Amanda Ribas. She is characterized by her physical strength and an ability to maintain a high wrestling tempo throughout a fifteen-minute bout.

Amanda Lemos remains one of the most dangerous power hitters in the history of the strawweight division, currently holding the record for the most knockdowns in the weight class. Operating primarily as a Counter Striker, she possesses a devastating left hand and high-impact leg kicks that can end a fight instantly. While her striking accuracy is among the highest in the top 10, she has recently struggled against elite-level grappling specialists. Despite these setbacks, she remains a significant 'spoiler' in the division; her physical strength in the clinch and her ability to find technical submissions like the guillotine make her a threat even when the fight leaves her preferred striking range.

Jéssica Andrade is the division's most prolific finisher and a former champion. Known for her 'Bate Estaca' (Pile Driver) style, she relies on immense physical strength and a high-volume approach. She is a powerhouse who excels in the clinch and is famous for high-amplitude slams. While her defensive striking has shown holes against technical counter-punchers lately, her ability to change the course of a fight with a single hook or a devastating body-shot flurry remains elite.

Iasmin Lucindo is the youngest fighter in the strawweight top 10 and represents the next generation of Brazilian talent. She is an aggressive athlete who favors a high-pressure approach, using heavy hooks and a powerful overhand right to keep her opponents on the defensive. Despite her youth, she possesses a sophisticated clinch game, often utilizing knees to the body to soften opponents before transitioning to takedowns. Her grappling is well-rounded and she is difficult to hold down.

personal-info.name: Marina Rodriguez
personal-info.fighting-out-of: Bagé, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil

description: Marina Rodriguez is a master of distance and one of the most technical exponents of Muay Thai in the women's divisions. She utilizes a long, snapping jab and a variety of piercing teep kicks to keep her opponents at the end of her reach. Her most lethal weapon is her clinch game; she is renowned for her 'Thai Plumb,' delivering devastating knees and elbows. While her defensive wrestling has historically been her weakness, she has made strides in her ability to remain upright. However, her 2025-2026 campaign has been challenging, dropping fights to high-pressure grapplers.

personal-info.name: Karolina Kowalkiewicz
personal-info.gym: American Top Team / Shark Top Team

description: Karolina Kowalkiewicz is a highly respected veteran and former title challenger known for her high-volume striking and sophisticated clinch game. She excels at maintaining a relentless pace, utilizing rapid knees and short elbows to disrupt her opponents. While she lacks one-punch knockout power, her accuracy and veteran savvy make her a difficult test for any contender. In late 2025 and 2026, she has transitioned into a high-level gatekeeper role, continuing to challenge the division's rising stars with her technical depth.

Piera Rodriguez is a relentless physical force in the strawweight division, known for her 'La Fiera' aggression. She primarily uses high-volume boxing to close the distance, where she then transitions into a strong clinch and takedown game. Her best work occurs on the ground, where she employs a heavy top-control style centered on grueling ground and pound. After navigating a tough stretch in 2024, she has rebounded in early 2026 with a significant technical victory over Sam Hughes, showcasing a more disciplined approach to her conditioning and cage-control.

Luana Pinheiro is an elite Judo black belt who brings explosive athleticism and high-amplitude hip tosses to the cage. While she possess dangerous power in her overhand right, her primary strength lies in her ability to dominate the clinch and dictate where the fight takes place. Despite a challenging run of results in 2024 and 2025 against elite volume strikers and grapplers, she remains a physically imposing threat in the first two rounds of any bout.

personal-info.name: Tecia Pennington
personal-info.gym: Rocky Mountain Masters / American Top Team

description: Tecia Pennington is the division's quintessential 'Technical Boxer,' known for having some of the fastest hands and highest volume in the weight class. A perennial top-15 fixture, she utilizes a movement-heavy style that makes her incredibly difficult to hit cleanly. While she lacks stopping power, her ability to out-point opponents over three rounds is elite. In 2025, she proved she still belongs at the top level with a tactical masterclass over the explosive Luana Pinheiro, though she recently dropped a competitive decision to the surging Denise Gomes in late 2025. Her veteran savvy and defensive wrestling remain her primary tools for neutralizing younger, more aggressive power hitters.

Denise Gomes has emerged as one of the strawweight division's most dangerous rising contenders by 2026. A physically imposing 'Pressure Striker,' she is known for her exceptional power and aggressive forward movement. Her 2025 campaign was particularly successful, highlighted by a technical victory over veteran Tecia Pennington and a clear decision over Karolina Kowalkiewicz. Gomes excels at trapping opponents against the fence and unleashing heavy combinations. While her grappling was once considered a vulnerability, she has shown significantly improved defensive wrestling and an ability to maintain top position, making her a dark horse for the title conversation in the coming year.

Alexia Thainara has rapidly ascended into the top 15 by early 2026, establishing herself as one of the most promising Brazilian prospects in the strawweight division. Training out of the legendary Chute Boxe, she utilizes a sophisticated Muay Thai base complemented by a dangerous submission game. Her 2025 and early 2026 runs were highlighted by technical dominance over high-level opposition, including a signature submission win over Molly McCann. Thainara is characterized by her calm composure and technical striking defense, often drawing opponents into traps before initiating clinical grappling transitions. As of March 2026, she is widely considered a future top-5 threat.

Valentina Shevchenko has fully reclaimed her status as the greatest female flyweight in history. After reclaiming her title from Alexa Grasso in late 2024, she spent 2025 turning back the division's strongest challengers. Her game remains the gold standard for technical proficiency, combining elite counter-striking with a stifling clinch and high-amplitude takedowns. Most recently, she solidified her 'Pound-for-Pound' #1 status with a dominant title defense against Manon Fiorot and a legacy-defining superfight victory over Strawweight queen Zhang Weili in November 2025. Entering 2026, she appears as invincible as ever.

Natalia Silva has established herself as the most elite technical striker in the flyweight division as of 2026. A prototypical 'Outside Sniper,' she utilizes world-class taekwondo-style footwork and a blistering lead leg to keep opponents at bay. Her 2025 and early 2026 campaigns were flawless, headlined by a tactical masterclass victory over former champion Alexa Grasso and a dominant unanimous decision over Rose Namajunas in January 2026. Silva possesses perhaps the best takedown defense in the division, allowing her to force technical kickboxing matches that she invariably wins on volume and precision. Currently ranked #1, she is the clear next challenger for Valentina Shevchenko's throne.

Alexa Grasso remains the elite technician of the flyweight division. After her historic trilogy with Valentina Shevchenko concluded in late 2024, she has remained a staple at the very top of the rankings. Her boxing is widely considered the crispest in the weight class, characterized by high-accuracy combinations and excellent footwork. In 2025, she faced a difficult challenge in Natalia Silva, dropping a competitive decision, but she remains one of the most dangerous fighters in the world due to her opportunistic submission game and high fight IQ. Entering 2026, she is looking to re-establish her path toward the title she once held.

Manon Fiorot is a physical powerhouse and an elite striker who utilizes her karate and kickboxing background to dominate opponents. She is known for her exceptional takedown defense, which as of 2026 remains nearly impenetrable, forcing opponents to engage with her on the feet. After falling short in a highly competitive title challenge against Valentina Shevchenko in May 2025, 'The Beast' rebounded emphatically in October 2025 with a first-round TKO victory over Jasmine Jasudavicius. Her style is defined by heavy sidekicks, crisp combinations, and a disciplined use of distance, making her a permanent fixture in the title conversation.

Erin Blanchfield is the premier grappling specialist of the flyweight division. Known for her 'Cold Blooded' efficiency, she excels at utilizing relentless chain wrestling to force opponents into deep water on the mat. Her top control is considered the most suffocating in the division, often leading to technical submissions or heavy ground and pound. After a setback against Manon Fiorot in 2024, she significantly improved her striking defense and returned to her dominant ways, most recently securing a brilliant rear-naked choke victory over Tracy Cortez in November 2025. At just 26 years old, she remains a foundational pillar of the division's elite.

Maycee Barber has matured into a top-tier powerhouse in the flyweight division. Known for her aggressive 'The Future' persona, she utilizes high-impact striking and a brutal clinch game to overwhelm opponents. In 2025, she secured a significant victory over Karine Silva, showcasing improved tactical patience and physical strength. While she suffered a setback in March 2026 against Alexa Grasso—losing by first-round knockout in a high-stakes rematch—she remains one of the division's most dangerous finishers. Her style is characterized by heavy elbows in the clinch and a relentless forward-moving pressure that forces opponents into defensive shells.

Rose Namajunas is a former two-time strawweight champion who has successfully transitioned into the elite of the flyweight division. Known for her exceptional technical footwork and 'Thug' Rose precision, she remains one of the most cerebral strikers in the sport. Her game is built on a world-class jab and high-level movement that frustrates aggressive opponents. While she has faced a mixed run in 2025 and early 2026—including a technical decision loss to the #1 ranked Natalia Silva in January 2026—her veteran savvy and dangerous submission threat from the bottom keep her ranked as a top-6 threat. Namajunas excels in fights where she can dictate the rhythm from the outside and exploit technical openings.

Jasmine Jasudavicius is a physically imposing flyweight who excels at utilizing her height and reach to initiate heavy clinch exchanges. A versatile 'All-Round Grappler,' she recently earned her BJJ black belt following a dominant submission victory over veteran Jessica Andrade in early 2025. Her game is centered on wearing opponents down with relentless pressure and superior top control. While her striking defense was tested in a TKO loss to Manon Fiorot in October 2025, her recent run—including a tactical win over Mayra Bueno Silva—has solidified her place as a Top-7 contender. She is widely considered one of the most durable and grind-heavy fighters in the division.

Tracy Cortez is a highly technical wrestler known for her exceptional scrambles and top-heavy control. She effectively uses a clean boxing game to close distance before initiating the clinch and dragging opponents to the mat. Throughout 2024 and 2025, she faced the division's absolute elite, including a high-profile main event against Rose Namajunas and a grueling battle with Erin Blanchfield in November 2025. While she has dropped decisions to the top-tier of the weight class, her durability and fundamental wrestling consistency make her a difficult out for any prospect. Entering 2026, she remains a top-10 staple focused on refining her striking power to complement her elite grappling base.

Karine Silva is one of the most clinical finishers in the flyweight division, currently tied for the third-most submissions in the weight class's history. A Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu black belt with high-level training from the 'Fighting Nerds' camp, she is a specialist at snatching necks, particularly favoring the guillotine and D'Arce choke. While she possesses genuine knockout power in her hands, her 2025 campaign saw a slight plateau as she struggled against elite-level volume strikers like Maycee Barber. Despite a recent loss in late 2025, her ability to end a fight instantly on the mat makes her a constant threat to the top 10. As of April 2026, she remains a formidable gatekeeper to the title tier.

Miranda Maverick is a physical powerhouse in the flyweight division, currently ranked #10 as of April 2026. A standout 'Wrestler,' she utilizes a high-octane southpaw volume-striking style to set up powerful double-leg takedowns. Maverick's greatest strength lies in her top-tier grappling and ground control, where she consistently hunts for submissions. Her 2025 campaign was highly successful, highlighted by a dominant unanimous decision over the durable Tracy Cortez and a late-round armbar finish over JJ Aldrich. While she has faced technical hurdles against top-5 counter-strikers in the past, her physical development and refined striking defense have made her a legitimate dark horse for a title run in 2026.

Casey O'Neill has successfully navigated a difficult recovery from ACL surgery to re-establish herself in the top 15 as of 2026. A relentless 'Pressure Striker' with high-output boxing, she thrives in high-paced brawls where she can overwhelm opponents with volume. Her 2025 campaign was a major turning point, highlighted by a dominant performance over Luana Santos. Most recently, in March 2026, she secured a tactical victory over Gabriella Fernandes, showcasing a more measured approach to her striking defense while maintaining her signature aggression. O'Neill's game is built on durable cardio and a punishing top-control game that wears opponents down over three rounds.

Wang Cong is widely regarded as one of the most dangerous strikers in the division, having gained legendary status for a kickboxing victory over Valentina Shevchenko prior to their MMA careers. By April 2026, she has surged into the top 12 following a dominant 2025 campaign. Her game is built on world-class boxing fundamentals, exceptional lateral movement, and a devastating counter-right hand. In February 2026, she solidified her contender status with a technical masterpiece against Eduarda Moura, showcasing significantly improved defensive wrestling. 'The Joker' represents a unique stylistic threat to the top 5 due to her high-level pedigree and ability to maintain a blistering pace over three rounds.

Eduarda Moura is a physically imposing grappling specialist known for her suffocating top pressure and aggressive ground-and-pound. Often drawing comparisons to early pioneers of the sport due to her 'Ronda' nickname and singular focus on the mat, she excels at bullying opponents against the fence and securing high-percentage takedowns. While her 2025 campaign highlighted some technical gaps in her striking defense—specifically in a high-profile loss to Wang Cong in early 2026—her ability to finish fights once they hit the canvas remains elite. As of April 2026, she is ranked #13 and remains a dangerous 'test' for any technical striker entering the top tier.

Viviane Araújo is a seasoned veteran who has long served as a high-level test for any rising flyweight. A Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu black belt with an exceptionally high-level jab, she often out-strikes opponents with technical boxing before utilizing her strong defensive wrestling to keep the fight standing. While she has faced a difficult run against the absolute elite, including a 2025 decision loss to Tracy Cortez, her win over Karine Silva in late 2024 proved she still has the physical tools to compete with the top 10. Heading into late 2026, she remains a durable, high-IQ gatekeeper to the title tier.

Julianna Peña reclaimed her throne as the Bantamweight Queen in late 2024 and has since defended it with her signature brand of 'unbreakable' pressure. Known for her relentless pace and world-class wrestling, she excels at breaking opponents mentally and physically in the later rounds. Her jab has become one of the best in the division, used effectively to set up her clinch entries. Throughout 2025, she solidified her legacy by defeating Raquel Pennington in a tactical five-round battle, proving that her grit and championship experience are currently unmatched at 135 pounds.

Raquel 'Rocky' Pennington remains one of the most durable and technically sound fighters in the history of the division. A former champion, her game is built on elite clinch work and a highly disciplined boxing base. She is notoriously difficult to finish, possessing a granite chin and exceptional defensive wrestling. While she lost her title to Julianna Peña in 2024, she remained a top contender through 2025, utilizing her veteran savvy to out-grind younger prospects. As of April 2026, she is the division's #1 ranked contender, serving as the ultimate litmus test for anyone eyeing a shot at the belt.

Kayla Harrison is arguably the most dominant physical force in the history of women's MMA. A two-time Olympic gold medalist in Judo, she has seamlessly transitioned her elite grappling to the octagon, where she remains undefeated in the UFC as of April 2026. Her 2025 campaign reached its pinnacle in June, where she secured a second-round Kimura victory over Julianna Peña to win the Bantamweight title (though current rankings as of early 2026 show a tight race at the top). Harrison’s game is defined by absolute control; once she initiates a clinch or secures a takedown, her opponents rarely find a way back to their feet. Her ground-and-pound is devastating, and her submission game is increasingly versatile, making her the division's 'final boss.'

Norma Dumont has surged into the Top 3 of the bantamweight division through clinical, disciplined performances. A Sanda specialist with exceptional technical boxing, Dumont excels at controlling distance and out-pointing opponents with superior accuracy and defensive movement. Her 2025 campaign was her most successful to date, highlighted by a high-stakes victory over Ketlen Vieira in November, which extended her winning streak to six. As of April 2026, she is scheduled to face Joselyne Edwards, with many viewing her as the next logical challenger for the title. Her game is characterized by physical strength, a heavy jab, and nearly impenetrable takedown defense.

Ketlen Vieira is a world-class Judoka and BJJ black belt who has remained a staple of the Bantamweight top five for years. Known for her physically imposing frame and excellent takedown defense, she often neutralizes opponents in the clinch and wins technical striking battles behind a stiff jab. Her 2025 campaign was challenging, suffering a narrow decision loss to Norma Dumont in a high-stakes contender match. However, Vieira remains one of the most difficult puzzles to solve in the division due to her strength and fundamental soundness. As of April 2026, she is looking to recalibrate and use her grappling pedigree to stop the momentum of the division's surging newcomers.

Macy Chiasson is the most physically daunting athlete in the Bantamweight division, standing at 5'11" with a massive reach advantage over almost every contemporary. A product of Fortis MMA, she has evolved into a lethal clinch fighter, utilizing her knees and elbows to devastating effect. Her 2025 run saw a significant career resurgence, most notably a dominant TKO victory over Pannie Kianzad that showcased her improved striking pressure. By early 2026, Chiasson has solidified her spot in the Top 5, proving that her unique combination of size and technical Muay Thai makes her a nightmare stylistic matchup for the division's shorter, boxing-centric contenders.

Ailín Pérez has emerged as the breakout star of the bantamweight division, combining elite wrestling with a massive social media presence. As of April 2026, 'Fiona' is ranked #6 and riding a significant five-fight win streak. Her game revolves around relentless takedown attempts and a suffocating top-control game that drains the energy of her opponents. In February 2026, she secured the biggest win of her career with a dominant decision over Macy Chiasson, proving her grappling can hold up against the division's most physically imposing athletes. While her striking continues to evolve at American Top Team, her ability to dictate where the fight takes place makes her a looming threat to the top five.

Irene Aldana remains one of the most respected and dangerous strikers in the bantamweight division. A technical boxer with legitimate one-punch knockout power, she is known for her crisp combinations and legendary durability, most notably shown in her 2024 'Fight of the Year' candidate against Karol Rosa and her gritty performance against Norma Dumont. While she has alternated wins and losses in recent years, her status as a high-level contender is undisputed. As of early 2026, Aldana continues to be the division's primary striking threat, often serving as a gatekeeper to the title picture for anyone who cannot handle her volume and precision on the feet.

Yana Santos is a highly experienced Muay Thai practitioner who has remained a Top 10 fixture for the better part of a decade. Known for her elite clinch-striking and relentless pace, she excels at wearing opponents down against the fence with knees and short elbows. After a difficult 2023-2024 stretch following her return from maternity leave, Santos found her rhythm again in late 2025, securing a vital unanimous decision over Macy Chiasson in October. Entering 2026, she is utilizing her high-level training at American Top Team to transition into a more veteran, tactically focused role, serving as a dangerous technical hurdle for the division's rising prospects.

Karol Rosa is arguably the most prolific volume striker in the bantamweight division, frequently setting records for significant strikes landed in three-round bouts. Her game is predicated on a relentless offensive output, particularly her devastating low kicks and crisp boxing combinations. Rosa is renowned for her incredible durability and 'fight of the night' style, as seen in her legendary 2024 clash with Irene Aldana. In August 2025, she solidified her Top 10 status with a gritty win over Nora Cornolle. As of April 2026, she is scheduled for a high-stakes matchup against Luana Santos in June, where she looks to use her veteran experience and superior cardio to break into the Top 5 conversation.

Jacqueline Cavalcanti has quickly ascended into the Top 10 as of April 2026, establishing herself as one of the division's most promising technical strikers. The former LFA champion utilizes a sharp, long-range jab and excellent lateral movement to frustrate opponents. Her 2025 campaign was perfect, highlighted by a technical masterclass victory over Julia Avila in February and a high-stakes decision win over Mayra Bueno Silva in November. Cavalcanti is currently scheduled for a massive title-eliminator bout against Ketlen Vieira in May 2026. Her style is characterized by high-volume point fighting and a nearly 90% takedown defense rate, making her a nightmare for traditional grapplers in the weight class.

Joselyne Edwards, known as 'La Pantera,' is a gritty and physically strong striker who thrives in close-quarters combat. After a somewhat inconsistent run in 2024, she significantly improved her conditioning and striking power throughout 2025. Her most notable achievement in the past year was a dominant first-round TKO victory over Chelsea Chandler in April 2025, followed by a hard-fought decision over Nora Cornolle in February 2026. Currently ranked #11, she is set for a pivotal clash against #3 ranked Norma Dumont later this month. Edwards is best known for her heavy leg kicks and a 'dog-fight' mentality that makes her a threat until the final bell, though her takedown defense remains the primary area for technical growth.

Mayra Bueno Silva is one of the most dangerous opportunistic submission threats in the division, specifically known for her lethal armbars and chokes. After a meteoric rise that led to a vacant title shot in early 2024, Silva has faced a grueling 2025 campaign, dropping decision losses to Jasmine Jasudavicius and Jacqueline Cavalcanti. Despite these setbacks, her ability to snatch a limb in a scramble remains second to none. Currently ranked #12 as of April 2026, she is scheduled to face Michelle Montague later this month, looking to snap a four-fight skid and prove her 'Sheetara' submission game is still top-tier caliber.

Nora Cornolle is a high-level Muay Thai specialist who has quickly made a name for herself as a powerful addition to the 135-pound division. Known for her brutal leg kicks and dominant clinch work, the French prospect secured a massive TKO victory over Melissa Mullins in early 2025. However, she has faced growing pains against the division's upper echelon, dropping recent decisions to Karol Rosa and Joselyne Edwards in early 2026. Currently ranked #13, Cornolle remains one of the division's hardest hitters. Her path forward relies on sharpening her defensive wrestling to ensure her elite striking can dictate the pace of the fight.

Luana Santos is one of the brightest young prospects in the Bantamweight division, currently ranked #14 as of April 2026. A high-level Judo black belt, her game is built on explosive hip tosses and a lethal submission game, particularly favoring the rear-naked choke. After a learning experience against Casey O'Neill in late 2024, she bounced back in 2025 with two dominant performances, including a submission win over Melissa Croden in December. Santos possesses a relentless pace and aggressive striking that she uses to initiate grappling exchanges. She is currently scheduled for her biggest test to date against veteran Karol Rosa in June 2026, a match many see as her opportunity to break into the Top 10.

Bia Mesquita entered the UFC with one of the most decorated grappling resumes in history, being a 10-time BJJ World Champion. As of April 2026, she has successfully broken into the #15 spot in the rankings. Her transition to MMA has been clinical; her ground game is widely considered the most technical in the division, featuring an unparalleled ability to find submissions from any position. In March 2026, she secured a signature victory over Montserrat Rendon, demonstrating that even against veteran opposition, her grappling is an 'auto-win' button once the fight hits the floor. While her striking remains a work in progress, her defensive savvy and elite BJJ make her one of the most avoided fighters in the bottom half of the Top 15.

Josh Hokit is a high-motor heavyweight whose game is built on elite athleticism and NCAA All-American wrestling. While he recently set volume records in his April 2026 win over Curtis Blaydes, his striking is characterized more by relentless output and physical conditioning than technical perfection. He often uses his striking as a vehicle to force clinches and takedowns where his wrestling can truly shine. Despite some defensive lapses and a lack of traditional technical 'finesse' in his boxing, his ability to maintain a lightweight-esque pace for three full rounds makes him a physical anomaly and a major problem for the aging heavyweight elite.

fight-history: W vs Gilbert Burns (KO/TKO - Strikes) R3 2:08 - UFC Fight Night 273

description: Mike Malott is a Canadian welterweight who recently scored a signature third-round TKO over former title challenger Gilbert Burns at UFC Winnipeg, improving his record and rising into the UFC welterweight rankings. Poised, patient pressure striker with improving all-round skills and growing momentum in the division.

A Moroccan-born, Sydney-based featherweight who relocated at 19 to pursue MMA with no prior experience in the sport, building his entire game from a kickboxing foundation. Rahiki is an explosive finisher with a 100% finishing rate across nine professional fights, all inside the distance. His striking is sharp and aggressive, with a knack for finding the finish quickly — both his UFC wins came in the first two rounds. His grappling is untested and underdeveloped at this stage of his career, representing the clearest area for growth as he faces higher-level opposition.

A London-born heavyweight with a rugby background who came up through British regional MMA before earning a UFC contract. Sutherland is a straightforward pressure striker who relies on physical intimidation and power punching to get finishes. His grappling defense is a significant liability — he was submitted in the first round by Valter Walker in his UFC debut via heel hook, exposing a takedown defense and ground game that needs significant work. He bounced back with a dominant decision win over a shot Tai Tuivasa, showing his striking can be effective against the right opponent.

An Australian heavyweight of Croatian heritage who trained with City Kickboxing in Auckland alongside Israel Adesanya. Pericic is a pure power striker with a 100% finishing rate, all three of his UFC wins coming in the first round. He carries exceptional one-shot knockout power and combines it with the technical striking foundation developed at City Kickboxing. His grappling is untested in the UFC but his takedown defense has been adequate so far. The sole loss on his record came earlier in his career before his City Kickboxing development. He has positioned himself as one of the most exciting heavyweight prospects in the promotion.

A New York-born, Auckland-based featherweight who trains at City Kickboxing under Eugene Bareman. Schmid holds a Taekwondo black belt and BJJ blue belt and won IMMAF Oceania gold at featherweight. He is a dangerous striker with knockout power in both hands, most notably finishing Jeffrey Mesa in 15 seconds with a spinning backfist. His UFC debut came on four days notice against undefeated prospect Marwan Rahiki at UFC Perth, a significant step up in competition he was unable to overcome. Still an early-career fighter with clear upside given his elite training environment.

One of the UFC's most beloved personalities, the Western Sydney-born heavyweight of Wiradjuri and Samoan heritage built his reputation on explosive knockout power and an infectious crowd-pleasing style. At his 2021-22 peak he strung together five consecutive finishes including victories over Derrick Lewis and Augusto Sakai. Since then he has suffered seven consecutive losses to elite competition, being finished by strikes, submissions and decisions. His grappling defense is a severe liability at the top level — he has been submitted multiple times in recent years — and his chin and durability have visibly declined. He remains dangerous in a phone booth brawl but is no longer competitive against ranked opposition.

Dooho Choi is one of the most purely explosive finishers the Featherweight division has ever seen. His 2016 war with Cub Swanson is an all-time classic, inducted into the UFC Hall of Fame Fight Wing in 2022. After mandatory South Korean military service caused a lengthy absence, Choi returned in 2023 and has looked reinvigorated, back-to-back TKO wins in 2024 proving his one-shot power has not faded. He is a pure Pressure Striker with almost no grappling ambition — he walks forward, throws bombs, and dares opponents to trade with him.

Juan Diaz is a Peruvian bantamweight who relocated to Tijuana to train at Entram Gym and built his reputation on the Latin American regional scene, winning the LUX Fight League Bantamweight title before earning a UFC contract on Dana White's Contender Series in October 2025 with the first spinning back elbow KO in DWCS history. Born in Peru and raised partly in Argentina, he carries all three flags with pride. His UFC debut comes at Allen vs. Costa on May 16, 2026 against Malcolm Wellmaker.

Born in Klaipėda, Lithuania and raised in England from age three, Modestas Bukauskas is a four-time British kickboxing champion and two-time Cage Warriors Light Heavyweight champion. His UFC run has been a rollercoaster of spectacular elbow KOs and sudden stoppages. A serious leg injury against Khalil Rountree Jr. led to his first UFC release, but he worked back through surgery and rebuilt his momentum impressively, winning four straight inside the Octagon before a third-round KO loss to Nikita Krylov in January 2026. A switch-stance Muay Thai specialist with genuine finishing power.

One of the most exciting young prospects in the Middleweight division, Ateba Gautier moved from Cameroon to Manchester at 19 to train under Lerone Murphy's gym, Manchester Top Team. Born in Douala, he earned a silver medal at the 2024 African Sambo Championships and was signed from Dana White's Contender Series after a second-round KO of Yura Naito. He has a 100% finishing rate with all nine of his stoppages coming via KO or TKO. Gautier's rapid UFC rise continued with a TKO win over Ozzy Diaz at UFC 328.

Grant Dawson is one of the most suffocating wrestlers in the Lightweight division and quietly one of the most underrated fighters on the UFC roster. Born in Cambria, Wisconsin and raised in Stromsburg, Nebraska, he built a 100% finishing rate on the regional scene before joining American Top Team in Florida. He uses his striking purely to initiate clinch work and takedowns, where his top control and submission hunting are elite — he has the record for ground control time in several of his UFC bouts. Most recently he submitted Mateusz Rebecki at UFC 328 in May 2026.

fight-history: W vs Zhang Mingyang (KO/TKO - Punches) R1 4:15 - UFC Fight Night: Song vs. Figueiredo

description: Born in Los Angeles and raised across 12 foster homes after losing his father young, Alonzo Menifield channeled his adversity into football and eventually MMA. He trains at Fortis MMA in Dallas and is one of the most dangerous first-round punchers in the Light Heavyweight division, carrying nuclear power in his hands. His career has been defined by explosive early finishes and sudden knockouts when the power lands. He is next scheduled to face Zhang Mingyang at UFC Fight Night Macao on May 30, 2026.
